repositories.brief_summary: Bug fixes for runtime events, flambda, type checker, and configure; DragonFly BSD support added; AR/ARFLAGS build system standardization.
repositories.new_features_summary: DragonFly BSD frame pointer support, AR/ARFLAGS build standardization, runtime_events hang fix, flambda stub inlining fix.
repositories.new_features:
- Fixed **infinite loop** in `runtime_events.c`'s `write_to_ring` when events exceed the configured ring buffer size — the runtime now terminates with a fatal error (e.g. `runtime_events: event of N words exceeds the ring buffer`) rather than hanging indefinitely, closing a long-standing bug first reported in 2024 ([#14834](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14834), fixes [#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13335))
- Fixed **warning 60 (unused-module) source location** for non-aliasable module substitutions — the warning now correctly points to the substitution site rather than the original module declaration in a different file, fixing confusing build-system diagnostics ([#14831](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14831), fixes [#12745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12745))
- Fixed **infinite recursive stub inlining** in flambda when optional argument defaults reference the enclosing function (e.g. `let rec f ?(x = f ()) () = x + 1`) — flambda no longer loops on such inputs ([#14839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14839), fixes [#14828](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14828))
- Added **DragonFly BSD frame pointer support** for AMD64, resolving assembler errors with gcc 8.3 and binutils 2.34 when building with `--enable-frame-pointers`; DragonFly BSD is now also recognized as BSD in ocamltest ([#14840](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14840))
- **Build system**: `AR` and `ARFLAGS` are now exported in `Makefile.config` and support being overridden at configure time (e.g. `./configure AR=llvm-ar ARFLAGS=rcvs`); `ARCMD` is deprecated as of OCaml 5.5 ([#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552))
- Fixed **type recovery error messages**: paths are now correctly deep-copied when the type recovery mechanism freezes type expressions for error messages, preventing later unification from silently changing module names used in earlier error messages ([#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824))
- Fixed **configure.ac** incorrect `break` statements that broke default-argument detection, resolving broken installation on OpenSUSE with the site-config package ([#14846](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14846), follow-up to [#14802](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14802))
- Corrected **singular/plural grammar** in warning 57 message (upstreamed from OxCaml) ([#14843](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14843))
- Improved **`ocamlc` and `ocamlopt` manual pages** with small fixes ([#14838](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14838), extending [#14684](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14684), [#14782](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14782))
repositories.activity_summary: Two new stdlib and type-checker PRs opened; active reviews on flambda, ephemeron GC, extensible IO, and C API proposals.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened**: [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) opened [#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848) proposing `Dynarray.stable_sort_sub`, `Dynarray.stable_sort`, and `Dynarray.sort` stdlib additions, benchmarked with three implementation variants; [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened [#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845) to fix the typechecker crash (`Fatal error: exception Ctype.Unify(_)`) reported in [#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842)
- **Runtime system**: [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) merged the runtime_events hang fix ([#14834](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14834)); [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has an open PR improving multi-domain ephemeron marking to avoid redundant rounds ([#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774)), with benchmarks showing ~14% speedup at 12 domains
- **Type system**: [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) merged the type recovery path-copying fix ([#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824)) and backported a related fix to the 5.5 branch ([#14841](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14841)); active investigation of the `Ctype.Unify(_)` typechecker regression ([#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842)) with contributions from [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron)
- **Compiler optimizations**: [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) has an open PR ([#14763](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14763)) to merge nested labelled applications in Lambda, eliminating spurious intermediate closures; [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) continues review on the multi-architecture `Iatomic_fetch_add` native codegen PR ([#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)) with [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) reviewing amd64, arm64, riscv, and power backends
- **C API**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has an open proposal ([#14814](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14814)) adding `caml_result_find_exception` to `mlvalues.h`, providing a safer way to inspect `caml_result` values for exceptions from C code
- **Platform and build**: [Michael Neumann](https://github.com/mneumann) merged DragonFly BSD frame pointer support ([#14840](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14840)); [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) merged the long-running `AR`/`ARFLAGS`/`LN_S` build standardization ([#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552)); [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) fixed the configure.ac regression ([#14846](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14846))
- **Closed without merging**: [Nikos Gorogiannis](https://github.com/ngorogiannis) submitted [#14847](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14847) by mistake (GC counter flush code belonging to a different project) and it was promptly closed
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussions on AST extension points for ppxlib, typechecker crash affecting downstream packages, and extensible IO channel design.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) updated [#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) on adding extension points to the AST to improve ppxlib's AST freeze strategy; [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) summarized developer meeting consensus: include an `extension` case for all syntactic categories without surface syntax initially, adding parsing/printing support later as needed
- [#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842) reports a typechecker regression (`Fatal error: exception Ctype.Unify(_)`) on trunk affecting downstream packages including dolmen and containers; [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) are actively debugging level-escape and abbreviation-expansion issues in modular explicit types; fix under review in [#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845)
- Discussion in [#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842) included [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) raising concerns about LLM-generated text in bug reports diluting useful diagnostic information, with a suggestion to add guidance to `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- [#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) (extensible IO channels by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube)) gathered developer meeting feedback on design questions including seekable channel handling, stdlib API surface, js_of_ocaml compatibility, and C FFI backward compatibility; soft consensus to allow extensible IO channels
- [#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733) (Goblint performance regression 4.14 vs 5.x) received updated benchmarks showing +9% CPU regression on 5.5 (improved from +14% in earlier measurements), with investigation into the GC shortcut optimization partially closing the gap
repositories.trends_summary: Continued OxCaml-to-upstream contributions, active type system correctness work around modular explicits, and multi-platform GC improvements.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml upstream contributions** continue with warning grammar corrections ([#14843](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14843)) and flambda fixes ([#14839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14839)) originating in the OxCaml fork finding their way into trunk
- **Modular explicit types** remain an active area of correctness work — multiple issues and PRs this week ([#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824), [#14841](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14841), [#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842), [#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845)) address edge cases in type-level path handling and functor argument scoping
- **Multi-domain GC tuning** is receiving sustained attention: ephemeron marking optimization ([#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774)), the Goblint performance investigation ([#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733)), and the mark-delay PR ([#13580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13580)) all reflect ongoing work to close the performance gap with OCaml 4.x on GC-heavy workloads
- **New contributors** are actively merging patches: [Ryan Stewart](https://github.com/RyanJamesStewart) fixed a long-standing warning location bug ([#14831](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14831)) and [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) has contributed two PRs this week (testsuite message improvement [#14844](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14844) and the new Dynarray.sort proposal [#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848))
repositories.contributors_summary: Nine contributors merged or opened code this week spanning runtime, type system, build system, platform support, and stdlib additions.
repositories.contributors:

- [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) diagnosed and fixed the runtime_events infinite loop ([#14834](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14834), fixes [#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13335)) and also provided observations on the [#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842) typechecker regression

- [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) merged the long-running `AR`/`ARFLAGS`/`LN_S` configure and Makefile standardization ([#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552)) and provided code review on [#14834](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14834)

repositories.brief_summary: Active week of PR contributions with seven open PRs covering API cleanup, new .install file sections, git config fixes, and tar library integration.
repositories.new_features_summary: No commits merged to master during this week; new features are in open PRs under review.
repositories.new_features:
- [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) adds `prefix` and `prefixexec` sections to `.install` files, enabling packages to install files relative to the switch prefix
- [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) introduces an `OpamTar` module using the `tar` OCaml library instead of the `tar` system tool for archive manipulation
- [#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6957) fixes `opam init --bare` to skip compiler selection, reducing unnecessary startup time
repositories.activity_summary: Seven PRs were open during the week spanning API cleanup, bug fixes for git config and filename handling, and new .install file fields.
repositories.activity:
- [#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6957) opened by [Kayo Tei](https://github.com/tuesdayjz): bugfix to avoid compiler setup for `opam init --bare`, fixing [#5815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5815)
- [#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956) opened by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou): adds exhaustive reftest for all `.install` file fields, complementing [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938)
- [#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6955) opened by [Dmitri Volkov](https://github.com/sporkl): conditionally apply opam-specific git config only when not doing a full fetch, fixing [#6451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6451)
- [#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954) opened by [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio): large API cleanup removing 366 unused exported elements found via dead code analysis (−1856 lines across 84 files)
- [#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6953) opened by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb): draft PR fixing filename `startswith` behaviour
- [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) (original work by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)): use `tar` library instead of `tar` tool, marked READY TO MERGE
- [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) by [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian): `prefix`/`prefixexec` sections for `.install` files, under active review by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)
- Issue [#6952](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6952) opened and closed by [Pierre Villemot](https://github.com/Halbaroth): wrong name format in `pin-depends` is silently ignored (resolved: warning already exists)
- Issue [#6951](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6951) opened by [Vasile Rotaru](https://github.com/vrotaru): cyclic upgrades and downgrades observed with `opam upgrade`
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on PR #6938 focused on code style, use of existing Pp utilities, and handling of .opam-switch path checks.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938): [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) reviewed `prefix`/`prefixexec` implementation, suggesting use of `Pp.check` and `OpamPathName` constants rather than inline string checks; [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) responded on conservative path validation trade-offs
- [#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6955): [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) welcomed the first-time contribution from [Dmitri Volkov](https://github.com/sporkl) and noted the team is focused on finalising the 2.6 alpha release
- [#6951](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6951): [Vasile Rotaru](https://github.com/vrotaru) reported a repeatable cycle of upgrades and downgrades between dune-ecosystem packages with opam 2.5.1
repositories.trends_summary: The week shows a push toward code quality: large-scale dead code removal, expanded test coverage for .install files, and library-based archive handling.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- API surface reduction: [#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954) removes 366 unused exported elements, reflecting a trend toward a smaller, cleaner public API ahead of 2.6
- Replacing system tool dependencies with OCaml libraries: [#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) swaps the `tar` CLI tool for the `tar` OCaml library, improving portability
- Expanding `.install` file capabilities: [#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938) and [#6956](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6956) together add new fields and comprehensive tests, suggesting planned improvements to the install system
- Focus on the 2.6 alpha release: maintainer comments indicate the team is actively working toward a 2.6 alpha milestone

repositories.brief_summary: Proposal to integrate ocamlgrep structural search into ocaml-lsp opened discussion on LSP protocol fit.
repositories.activity_summary: An automated Nix flake lock update PR remains open alongside a newly filed ocamlgrep integration proposal.
repositories.activity:
- **Nix flake dependency update** via automated PR [#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) remains open, refreshing `merlin5_3` (to commit `653fdaaf` from 2026-06-02) and `nixpkgs` (to `nix-overlays` from 2026-06-06)
- **Ocamlgrep integration proposal** opened as [#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607) by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon), requesting feedback from maintainers on adding **structural OCaml search** backed by typed ASTs (`.cmt` files) as a custom LSP request
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion on whether ocamlgrep's grep-like functionality is a good fit for the LSP protocol.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened [#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607) proposing integration of **ocamlgrep** — a structural search tool that queries typed ASTs — into ocaml-lsp via a custom LSP request, linking a [VSCode demo](https://github.com/mjambon/ocamlgrep-vscode-demo) and a pre-draft PR; the work is being done for LexiFi under [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb)'s supervision
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) questioned the fit in [#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607), noting that ocaml-lsp focuses on exposing functionality through the LSP protocol and that a **grep-like utility** does not obviously align with that protocol's design — comparing it to wrapping `ripgrep` inside ocamllsp
repositories.trends_summary: Discussion reflects ongoing scrutiny of which developer tools belong inside the LSP server versus standalone.
repositories.emerging_trends: - The **ocamlgrep proposal** ([#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)) highlights a recurring question in the OCaml tooling ecosystem: whether whole-project analysis features should live inside the LSP server or remain independent tools; the maintainer response suggests a preference for keeping ocaml-lsp focused on the LSP protocol rather than becoming a general-purpose IDE backend
repositories.contributors_summary: Martin Jambon opened an integration proposal; Rudi Grinberg provided maintainer feedback on protocol fit.
repositories.contributors:
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) opened issue [#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607) proposing ocamlgrep integration, including a demo repository and a pre-draft PR with an initial implementation
- [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) responded to [#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607) with maintainer feedback on the suitability of the proposed approach relative to the LSP protocol's scope

repositories.brief_summary: Intensive memo engine refactoring and new API additions, major doc clarification push, Melange improvements, and several platform/correctness fixes.
repositories.new_features_summary: New %{git-sha} variable, pkg-config --static support, expanded enabled_if vars, Melange conditional rule generation, and parallel dep restoration.
repositories.new_features:
- Added `%{git-sha}` rule variable that expands to the short git SHA of the current HEAD commit, or empty string when git is unavailable ([#14810](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14810), [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots))
- `dune-configurator`: added optional `?static` argument to `Configurator.V1.Pkg_config.get` to instruct pkg-config to retrieve static linking options ([#14947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14947), [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust))
- **enabled_if expansion**: `(enabled_if)` in `(executable ...)` and `(install ...)` stanzas now allows any available variable, matching the behavior of `(library ...)` and other stanzas ([#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14860), fixes [#14789](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14789), [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan))
- **Melange**: Melange rules for multi-mode libraries are now only generated when `melc` is available in the switch, avoiding build errors for native-only setups ([#15021](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15021), [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro))
- **Memo engine**: `feat(memo)` — restored parallel dependency cutoffs concurrently rather than serially; the `cutoffs_that_reduce_concurrency_in_watch_mode` knob is removed ([#15012](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15012), [Andrey Mokhov](https://github.com/snowleopard))
- **Memo API additions**: new `Memo.create_rec` for self-recursive memoized functions, `Memo.Var.Unit`, pre-sizing memo table stores, `Invalidation.custom` and `to_reason_list`, node life-cycle events via `on_event`, and `reset_if_necessary` with non-reproducible error tracking ([#14960](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14960), [#14965](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14965), [#14967](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14967), [#14987](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14987), [#14973](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14973), [#14990](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14990), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- Added `--error-reporting=twice` separator: dune now prints an `==== Error Summary ====` header before the repeated error list, omitted when there are no errors ([#15016](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15016), fixes [#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14757), [Yahya Ehsan](https://github.com/yussypu))
- Dune version bumped to 3.25 on the main branch ([#14872](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14872))
- Added code-review skills for the Claude agent in `.claude/skills/` ([#14873](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14873), [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
- New how-to guide for organizing multiple executables in separate directories ([#14944](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14944), Chizy)

repositories.trends_summary: The memo subsystem is undergoing deep modularisation; concurrent dependency restoration marks a shift toward higher parallelism in watch mode.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The `Memo` module is being systematically decomposed into focused sub-modules (`node.ml`, `exec.ml`, `parallel.ml`, `deps_collector.ml`, `value.ml`) with explicit interfaces, signalling a broader effort to make the incremental computation engine easier to extend and reason about
- Concurrent restoration of parallel dependency cutoffs ([#15012](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15012)) removes a known serialisation bottleneck in watch mode, pointing toward more aggressive parallelism in future builds
- A sustained campaign to close long-standing documentation issues (many dating back to 2020–2022) substantially improves first-time user experience, indicating a quality-of-life focus alongside the engine work
- Melange integration is being tightened: conditional rule generation based on `melc` availability and disabling legacy ReScript support reduce noise for native-only projects
- Anonymous actions for odoc and C compiler detection (continued from prior weeks) reduces rule overhead and improves cache hit rates

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion on implicit source positions RFC (#52) following developer meeting consensus on abstract location type.
repositories.activity_summary: PR #52 on implicit source positions received a comment reporting developer meeting consensus on using a single abstract location type.
repositories.activity: - [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) posted a comment on the ongoing **implicit source positions** RFC ([#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) reporting that the last developer meeting reached consensus on using a single, abstract location type whose performance characteristics can be altered without changing the API, with plans to update the upstream implementation in [ocaml/ocaml#13886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13886) accordingly
repositories.discussions_summary: Developer meeting consensus reached on abstract location type design for implicit source positions RFC (#52).
repositories.notable_discussions: - The **implicit source positions** RFC ([#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) saw discussion following a developer meeting where the design question of location type representation was debated; [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) noted that [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) (the Jane Street advocate for option "1a + 2x") was absent, making the discussion one-sided, but consensus was reached that a single abstract `location` type is acceptable, with updates planned for [ocaml/ocaml#13886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13886)
repositories.contributors_summary: Olivier Nicole commented on RFC #52 reporting developer meeting outcomes on implicit source positions design.
repositories.contributors: - [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) (Tarides) commented on the **implicit source positions** RFC ([#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) summarizing developer meeting outcomes and announcing plans to update the upstream OCaml implementation ([ocaml/ocaml#13886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13886)) to use a single abstract location type

repositories.brief_summary: OxCaml zero_alloc annotation support merged, plus OpenBSD portability fixes and follow-up zero_alloc arity refinement PR opened.
repositories.new_features_summary: OxCaml `@@zero_alloc` annotation now rendered in generated documentation, closing issue #1418.
repositories.new_features:
- Support for **OxCaml `@@zero_alloc` annotations** merged by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) in [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), closing [#1418](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1418). The new `Zero_alloc` model type (with `opt`, `strict`, `arity`, and `custom_error_msg` fields) is added to `Lang.Value`, and the renderer now displays `@@zero_alloc` attributes on value signatures. Both `@@zero_alloc strict` and `@@zero_alloc opt` variants are supported, with full HTML, LaTeX, man, and Markdown output updated.
- **`ext_attrs` field** added to `Lang.Value.t` to carry OxCaml-specific value attributes such as `zero_alloc` through the documentation pipeline ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), [`af26831`](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/commit/af26831b2a3f28ae2eccdfd145b4f04131b6fd3d)).
repositories.activity_summary: Two new PRs opened: ppx_expect removal and zero_alloc arity refinement; two PRs merged covering zero_alloc support and OpenBSD portability.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened: removal of `ppx_expect`** from the test infrastructure by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) in [#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445) (open, not yet merged). This is a large change touching 65 files (10k additions, 9k deletions) that replaces `ppx_expect`-style tests while preserving test coverage.
- **Newly opened: zero_alloc arity display refinement** by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) in [#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444) (open, not yet merged). This follow-up to [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422) proposes omitting the arity from `@@zero_alloc arity N` when the arity matches the function's own arity, making the annotation more informative when printed.
- **OxCaml `@@zero_alloc` support merged** in [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV), closing the tracking issue [#1418](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1418). Reviewed by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) and [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd).
- **OpenBSD portability fixes** for cram tests merged in [#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1442) by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV). Replaced GNU `grep --context=<NUM>` with `-C<NUM>` (compatible with Busybox too), and replaced non-portable `sed \n` patterns with `awk`. Reviewed by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) and [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd).
- **OCaml 5.5 support issue closed** ([#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)): confirmed resolved by odoc 3.2.1 shipping in opam-repository, following the earlier [#1406](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1406) fix. Noted by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) and confirmed by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd).
- **OxCaml `kind_` definition support** tracked in newly opened [#1443](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1443) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w), added to the OxCaml tracking issue [#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411). The issue notes that while abbreviation usage is already rendered, the `kind_` definition itself and links from an abbreviation to its definition are still missing.
repositories.discussions_summary: OxCaml zero_alloc arity display discussed between contributors, with consensus on deferring the refinement to a separate follow-up PR.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- In [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) and [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) discussed whether the `arity N` argument of `@@zero_alloc arity N` should be omitted when it equals the function's visible arity. They agreed this makes sense but deferred it to [#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444) to keep the initial PR focused.
- In [#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1442), the team discussed cross-platform `sed`/`awk` portability in cram tests, settling on `-C<NUM>` grep syntax and `awk` for sed substitutions to support OpenBSD, Busybox, and GNU environments simultaneously.
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on OxCaml feature support, with zero_alloc done and kind_ definitions and arity refinement as next steps.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **OxCaml support initiative** (tracked in [#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)) is progressing steadily: `zero_alloc` annotations are now complete ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)), and `kind_` definitions ([#1443](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1443)) are next in the queue.
- **Cross-platform CI coverage** is expanding: fixes for OpenBSD in [#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1442) follow a pattern of making the test suite portable across BSD, GNU, and Busybox environments, consistent with ongoing portability work seen in other OCaml toolchain projects.
- The proposed **removal of `ppx_expect`** from the test suite ([#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445)) would reduce a development dependency and simplify the test infrastructure.
repositories.contributors_summary: Marek Kubica drove both merged PRs; Jon Ludlam and Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac provided reviews; art-w filed a new OxCaml issue.
repositories.contributors:
- [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) merged the OxCaml `@@zero_alloc` annotation support ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) and the OpenBSD cram test portability fix ([#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1442)), and opened the zero_alloc arity refinement PR ([#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444)).
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) reviewed and merged [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422) and [#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1442), and opened the `ppx_expect` removal PR ([#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445)).
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) provided detailed review feedback on [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422) (CPPO structure, code style) and [#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1442) (sed/awk correctness), and confirmed the OCaml 5.5 support issue ([#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)) as resolved.
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) opened the new tracking issue [#1443](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1443) for OxCaml `kind_` definition support.
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) originally filed the OCaml 5.5 support issue ([#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)), which was closed this week.

repositories.brief_summary: OCaml Workshop 2026 pre-event mini-site launched with CFP and committee pages; dune 3.24.0 alpha releases indexed.
repositories.new_features_summary: New /ocaml-workshop-2026 mini-site with three routes (home, CFP, committee) and opt-out show_header on Layout.base.
repositories.new_features:
- New **OCaml Workshop 2026 pre-event mini-site** added by [Cuihtlauac Alvarado](https://github.com/cuihtlauac), introducing three new routes: `/ocaml-workshop-2026` (home), `/ocaml-workshop-2026/cfp` (Call for Talks), and `/ocaml-workshop-2026/committee` (Program Committee), each with a bespoke workshop layout featuring the ocaml.org logo, tab navigation, a "Submit a Talk" CTA, and a footer theme toggle ([#3670](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3670))
- New `show_header` opt-out parameter added to `Layout.base` in `src/ocamlorg_frontend/layouts/layout.eml`, allowing workshop pages to suppress the standard top-nav header while leaving all other pages unaffected ([#3670](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3670))
- Three new URL constants added to `src/global/url.ml` (`ocaml_workshop_2026`, `ocaml_workshop_2026_cfp`, `ocaml_workshop_2026_committee`) and corresponding routes wired in `src/ocamlorg_web/lib/router.ml` ([#3670](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3670))
- **Dune 3.24.0 alpha1 and alpha2** release notes indexed under `data/platform-releases/dune/` via the automated platform-releases scraper ([#3671](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3671))
repositories.activity_summary: Open PR for Backstage OCaml Wasm/WASI tutorial under active review; automated scrapers brought in new Planet posts and platform release data.
repositories.activity:
- **Open PR under review**: [sabine](https://github.com/sabine) opened a Backstage OCaml article on the OCaml WASI prototype ([#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667)), receiving detailed technical feedback from [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) covering Wasm 3.0 standardisation status, `--effects=cps` support, updated `exnref`-based exception handling defaults, and current implementation metrics (5200 lines / 90 files)
- **OCaml Planet** automated scraper merged three batches of new community blog posts, bringing in content from robur.coop, anil, cwn, and tunbury ([#3668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3668), [#3669](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3669), [#3672](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3672))
- **Platform releases** scraper added dune 3.24.0 alpha1 and alpha2 draft release notes to the site ([#3671](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3671))
repositories.discussions_summary: Technical review on PR #3667 covers Wasm 3.0 standardisation, effects support, and updated WASI tooling defaults.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) provided substantive corrections on [#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667) (Backstage OCaml WASI article): clarifying that Wasm GC, tail calls, and exception handling are standardised in Wasm 3.0 (engine support still maturing), that `--effects=cps` remains available, that `exnref`-based exception handling is now the default for WASI binaries (removing the need for `--enable exnref`), and that `wizeng` no longer requires `-ext:legacy-eh`; also noting that the implementation has grown to 5200 lines across 90 files and that PR [ocaml/ocaml.org#2189](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/2189) had been merged the prior week
repositories.trends_summary: Bespoke event mini-sites emerging as a pattern for OCaml community events hosted outside the usual ICFP/conf.researchr.org infrastructure.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **OCaml Workshop 2026 mini-site** ([#3670](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3670)) establishes a pattern for hosting event pre-sites directly on ocaml.org when the usual conference infrastructure (conf.researchr.org) is unavailable — a likely recurring need as OCaml events diversify their host venues
- Deliberate **soft-launch approach**: the workshop pages are reachable by URL but not yet linked from the main navigation, with wiring deferred until co-chairs confirm readiness, suggesting an emerging practice of staged content rollout on ocaml.org

repositories.brief_summary: Releases include raylib 2.0.0, catala 1.2.0, awso 0.9.1, and awa 0.6.1; Windows CI testing of edited packages being hardened.
repositories.new_features_summary: New packages: raylib 2.0.0 game bindings, catala 1.2.0 law language, awso 0.9.1 AWS SDK, lun 0.0.3, awa 0.6.1 SSH fix.
repositories.new_features:
- **raylib 2.0.0** — three new package versions (`raylib.2.0.0`, `raygui.2.0.0`, `raylib-callbacks.2.0.0`) published by [Tobias Mock](https://github.com/tjammer), providing OCaml bindings for the Raylib game development framework; `qcaml` compatibility fixes were also merged to cope with API changes in this release ([#29978](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29978))
- **catala 1.2.0** — three packages (`catala.1.2.0`, `catala-format.1.2.0`, `catala-lsp.1.2.0`) published by [vbot](https://github.com/vincent-botbol), updating the law-as-code specification language and its LSP server ([#29983](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29983))
- **awso 0.9.1** — seven-package AWS OCaml SDK release (`awso`, `awso-async`, `awso-cli`, `awso-common`, `awso-eio`, `awso-lwt`, `awso-sync`) by [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) ([#29967](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29967))
- **lun 0.0.3** and **ppx_lun 0.0.3** — new versions of the `lun` library and its PPX companion by [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure), with documentation and website links fixed for prior versions too ([#29984](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29984))
- **awa 0.6.1** — two packages (`awa.0.6.1`, `awa-mirage.0.6.1`) for the pure-OCaml SSH implementation, fixing parsing of `MSG_KEX_n` and `MSG_USERAUTH_n` messages, by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) ([#30001](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30001))
- **object 0.1** — new OCaml library package published by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) ([#29973](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29973))
- **patricia-tree 0.14.0** — new release by [Dorian Lesbre](https://github.com/dlesbre) ([#29982](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29982))
- **ppx_inline_test_nobase v0.17.0.3** — release by [Dmitrii Kosarev](https://github.com/Kakadu), with a constraint on `ppx_expect_nobase` added for the new version ([#29979](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29979))
- **ease-caml 0.1.5** — new version published by [Christopher Sumnicht](https://github.com/thyrgle) ([#29981](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29981))
- **vsrocq-language-server 2.4.3+1** — patch release fixing an incorrect `extra-source` path in the opam file, merged by [Enrico Tassi](https://github.com/gares) ([#29992](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29992))
- **bigstringaf 0.10.0** — corrected SHA-256 and SHA-512 hashes (previously only MD5 was correct) by [Robin](https://github.com/robinanterior) ([#29977](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29977))
- **caqti-lwt** upper bound on Lwt relaxed by [@funwithcthulhu](https://github.com/funwithcthulhu) ([#29937](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29937))
repositories.activity_summary: New PRs include liquid-ml 0.1.4, migra 1.0.0, dune 3.24 alpha2, and awskit 0.1.0; Windows CI and dune 3.24 compatibility work is ongoing.
repositories.activity:
- **liquid-ml 0.1.4** (5 packages: `liquid_interpreter`, `liquid_ml`, `liquid_parser`, `liquid_std`, `liquid_syntax`) — Shopify Liquid template engine in OCaml with whitespace-control support and improved time handling, opened by [Ben Faerber](https://github.com/benfaerber) ([#30004](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30004)); the 0.1.3 submission was closed in favour of this updated release ([#30003](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30003))
- **migra 1.0.0** — first stable release of a database migration tool for OCaml supporting PostgreSQL, MariaDB, and SQLite, with checksum validation, out-of-order detection, `--dry-run` flag, and a library facade, opened by [David Sinclair](https://github.com/dsincl12) ([#30002](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30002))
- **dune 3.24.0~alpha2** (18 packages) — alpha pre-release of the dune build system submitted by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder), superseding the alpha0 submission which was closed ([#29996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29996), [#29969](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29969))
- **awskit 0.1.0** (10 packages) — new AWS infrastructure library with Eio and Lwt adapters by [Abdullah Elsayed](https://github.com/abdufelsayed), covering signing, credentials, endpoints, and core types ([#29980](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29980))
- **melange-edn 0.1.0** — EDN reader and writer for Melange, converting between EDN values and JavaScript JSON values, opened by [rcmerci](https://github.com/RCmerci) ([#29999](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29999))
- **Timedesc 3.1.2** (6 packages) — new release of the OCaml date-time handling library by [Darren Li](https://github.com/darrenldl); a 3.1.1 submission was closed and superseded by this version ([#29995](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29995), [#29994](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29994))
- **duras 2.0.0** — release opened by [Sergiy Duras](https://github.com/sduras) ([#29997](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29997))
- **Windows CI hardening** — [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened a PR to test edited packages differently from newly added ones: existing package versions are installed first, the switch locked, then upgraded to the PR version, catching regressions and lockfile breakage; includes pruning of extraneous software from GitHub Actions PATH for cleaner depext testing ([#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29998))
- **conf-pkgconf revert** — a Windows conf-package simplification ([#29976](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29976)) was merged then immediately reverted ([#29987](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29987)) after it was found to break lockfiles; [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) separately opened and then closed a more conservative fix attempt ([#29986](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29986))
- **dune 3.24 compatibility** — [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) merged multiple fixes: adding `dune < 3.24` upper bounds to all `coq` and `rocq` packages using deprecated `lang coq` or `using coq` stanzas ([#29988](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29988)), disabling mdx test-running for currently published versions ([#29989](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29989)), and then re-enabling tests conditionally for dune versions below 3.24 ([#29993](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29993))
- **Rocq 9.2 Makefile fix** — [Enrico Tassi](https://github.com/gares) fixed the Makefile target needed to build with Rocq 9.2 ([#29991](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29991))
- **Ongoing PRs from prior weeks** still awaiting merge include `server-reason-react.0.5.0` by [David Sancho](https://github.com/davesnx) ([#29631](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29631)), `bin_there.0.2.1a2` and `ppx_bin_there.0.2.1a2` by [David Teller](https://github.com/Yoric) ([#29807](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29807)), `lstar-rocq` by [Charles Averill](https://github.com/CharlesAverill) ([#29883](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29883)), and LLVM 20/21 releases by [Alan](https://github.com/alan-j-hu) ([#29110](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29110)) and `ocaml-option-llvm` by [Sora Morimoto](https://github.com/smorimoto) ([#29582](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29582))
repositories.discussions_summary: Lockfile invalidation from in-place conf-* mutations sparked policy debate; a proposal for static-linking option packages drew ecosystem-wide discussion.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Lockfile invalidation from mutating `conf-*` packages** — [Yosef](https://github.com/yosefAlsuhaibani) reported in [#29985](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29985) that in-place changes to `conf-*` dependency requirements (requiring `conf-pkg-config >= 5`) broke existing `opam lock` lockfiles and broke CI/Windows builds for downstream projects. [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) acknowledged the breakage and reverted the change ([#29987](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29987)), while noting that locking virtual packages like conf packages is not fully supported by opam's design.
- **Proposal for `opam-packages-option-static-linking`** — [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) opened [#29975](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29975) proposing new convention packages (similar to `ocaml-options`) to signal static linking preference across the ecosystem. The discussion drew in [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), and [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure), who shared experience from the MirageOS unikernel toolchain; Raphaël published a proof-of-concept branch and a dune-configurator patch as a demo.
repositories.trends_summary: dune 3.24 compatibility work is forcing upper bounds across coq/rocq/mdx packages; Windows CI testing infrastructure is being hardened.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **dune 3.24 compatibility cascade** — the upcoming dune 3.24 release removes the `lang coq` stanza and changes how `mdx` test targets work, requiring upper bounds on existing packages and conditional test configurations; this affected multiple coq, rocq-runtime, and mdx packages this week ([#29988](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29988), [#29989](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29989), [#29993](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29993), [#29996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29996))
- **Windows CI infrastructure investment** — [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) continues to invest in making Windows testing more reliable and catch real-world regressions, with PR [#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29998) introducing upgrade-path testing for edited packages and PATH pruning of pre-installed tools that could mask depext failures
- **Lockfile semantics and in-place package mutations** — the conf-pkgconf revert ([#29987](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29987)) and the issue discussion ([#29985](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29985)) illustrate ongoing tension between updating existing package metadata in-place versus publishing new versions, particularly for virtual/conf packages that get locked by end users

repositories.brief_summary: Merged `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` bindings (#2070); documentation migration to odoc/wodoc opened as draft PR (#2261).
repositories.new_features_summary: New `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` OCaml bindings added with format, formatToParts, and resolvedOptions methods.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`Intl.RelativeTimeFormat`** module added to `lib/js_of_ocaml/intl.ml` and `intl.mli`, providing OCaml bindings for the [ECMAScript Internationalization `RelativeTimeFormat` API](https://tc39.es/ecma402/#relativetimeformat-objects). Exposes `format` (returns a localised string), `formatToParts` (returns structured `format_part` array), and `resolvedOptions` methods, along with a typed `options` constructor accepting `localeMatcher`, `numberingSystem`, `style`, and `numeric` properties. The `relativeTimeFormat_constr` value is exported for direct `new%js` construction. ([#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070), [930c6b7](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/930c6b7f4126eff48cef94d5dd30d1078821eb62))
- **`API-STATUS.md`** updated to reflect `RelativeTimeFormat` coverage under the `Intl` entry, and **`CHANGES.md`** updated with the release note for [#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070). ([930c6b7](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/930c6b7f4126eff48cef94d5dd30d1078821eb62))
repositories.activity_summary: Documentation infrastructure overhaul opened as draft; wasm/compiler optimisation PRs remain under active review.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened (draft)**: [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) opened [#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261) to migrate the js_of_ocaml documentation pipeline from the wiki format to `odoc` + `wodoc`, replacing `manual/*.wiki` files with `.mld` sources, removing the hand-written build scripts and Python tools, and publishing via a single `dune build @doc` step themed with the Ocsigen site chrome. The PR also updates the GitHub Actions `doc.yml` workflow and repurposes `tools/check_manual_links.sh` to check only external links (internal references now validated by odoc at build time).
- **CSS Font Loading API** binding awaits review as [#2255](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2255) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo), adding `Css_font_loading.fontFace` / `fontFaceSet` class types and a `fonts` property on `Dom_html.document`.
- **Runtime `uncaughtException` handler scoping** fix open as [#2258](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2258) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo), limiting the Node.js handler to standalone programs only (resolves [#1277](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1277)).
- **Compiler JS minification** enhancements in [#2257](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2257) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo): esbuild-style cosmetic transforms in compact mode — `!0`/`!1` for booleans, stripped leading zeros for decimal fractions, normalised exponents, and backtick-quoted strings where they reduce escape count.
- **Wasm optimisation** work ongoing: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) has several draft PRs under development — skipping `wasm-opt --opt 1` in favour of targeted local-variable optimisations ([#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238)), exception handling optimisations ([#2098](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2098)), WASI runtime support ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), static evaluation experiments ([#2153](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2153)), and a GC-after-parsing memory reduction ([#2259](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2259)).
- **Closed without merge**: [#1759](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1759) (experiment with new Wasm exception handling instructions) closed by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) as the instructions are still behind a flag in Chrome and Node.js.
repositories.discussions_summary: Active review on documentation migration PR #2261 between hhugo and Vincent Balat; release 6.4 tracking issue updated.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261) (documentation migration) saw active back-and-forth: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) requested updates to `check_manual_links.sh` and the ability to preview the site locally; [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) addressed both, adding a `--local` flag to `wodoc build` that fetches shared CSS/image assets for offline rendering and repurposing the link-check script to external-only checks.
- Release tracking issue [#2241](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2241) (js_of_ocaml 6.4, targeting the OCaml 5.5 release) was updated on 2026-06-04, with [#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070) now merged and several other tracked PRs still open.
repositories.trends_summary: Steady expansion of browser/web API bindings and modernisation of the documentation toolchain.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **browser API binding surface** continues to grow incrementally — `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` merged this week ([#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070)), CSS Font Loading API pending ([#2255](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2255)), following the earlier popover API addition ([#1734](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1734)).
- **Documentation infrastructure** is being modernised from a custom wiki/Python pipeline to the standard OCaml `odoc` toolchain augmented by `wodoc`, aligning js_of_ocaml with other Ocsigen projects and with ocaml.org rendering ([#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261)).
- **Wasm backend** remains the focus of substantial ongoing optimisation work by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon), with multiple draft PRs targeting compilation speed ([#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238)), peak memory ([#2259](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2259)), exception handling ([#2098](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2098)), and WASI support ([#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)).
repositories.contributors_summary: Four contributors active: @toastal authored the merged Intl.RelativeTimeFormat PR; Jérôme Vouillon reviewed and merged it; Vincent Balat and @hhugo collaborated on the documentation migration draft.
repositories.contributors:
- [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) authored [#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070), implementing the `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` OCaml bindings that were merged this week.
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) reviewed [#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070) (requesting `API-STATUS.md` update and a placement fix), then merged it. repositories.brief_summary: A flurry of typing fixes for unboxed records, GADT kinds, and mode morphisms landed alongside the match-in-match flambda2 optimisation and a frametable relocation.
repositories.new_features_summary: The match-in-match heuristic and normal-form mode morphisms shipped, frametables moved to `.rodata` by default, and fexpr inspection was added to toplevel expect tests.
repositories.new_features:
- The long-running **match-in-match heuristic** [#4374](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4374) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) merged into flambda2, enabling continuation specialisation across nested match expressions and exposed via a new `-flambda2-match-in-match` flag with an accompanying `cont_spec_threshold` (replacing the old integer `cont_spec_budget`).
- **Normal-form morphisms** [#5453](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5453) landed, refactoring the mode solver's internal representation and unlocking more precise mode error hints via concrete modality propagation.
- Frametables are now placed in the **`.rodata` section** by default [#6094](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6094) by [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski), separating instruction and data pages to improve iTLB efficiency per Intel optimisation guidance.
- **`%%expect_fexpr`** annotations [#5837](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5837) by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev) can now appear in toplevel expect tests, allowing raw, simplified, and reaper-stage flambda2 IR to be snapshot-tested inline.
- A new **`OXCAML_RUNTIME`** C preprocessor symbol [#6203](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6203) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) gives external stubs a single canonical way to detect an OxCaml runtime, replacing the old patchwork of `CAML_RUNTIME_5`, `OCAML_5_MINUS`, etc.
repositories.activity_summary: A dense release day on 2026-06-05 saw the 5.2.0minus-40 magic number bump alongside a batch of typing and flambda2 correctness fixes, plus active upstream 5.4 merge work.
repositories.activity:
- [George Shammas](https://github.com/georgyo) opened [#6213](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6213) updating nixpkgs to 26.05 with an LLVM 21 build fix (superseding the auto-generated [#6212](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6212)).

- [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) merged [#6209](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6209) fixing a follow-on from [#6177](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6177) where `[@@unsafe_allow_any_mode_crossing]` declarations were rejected due to a stale env during topological updates.

repositories.discussions_summary: Discussions centred on variant unboxing being too aggressive, the ergonomics of unique ownership with read accessors, and design tensions in the box type operator.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) opened [#6180](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6180) noting that flambda2 eagerly unboxes variant fields even when no local allocation is present, preventing an identity switch optimisation; the proposed fix is to only unbox fields that already have at least one use with the field available.
- [#6146](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6146) (now closed) asked for the recommended idiom for pairing `@ unique` destroy with interleaved read operations on C handles; the thread concluded that with-resource combinators or consume-and-return threading are the current options and borrowing is not yet available.
- The long-running **box types** PR [#5342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5342) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) saw continued back-and-forth with [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) reaching near-approval, with open questions around GADT exhaustiveness and `mcomp` aliasability checks for `_ box`.
- [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) raised a concern in [#6177](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6177) about topological ordering as a long-term solution to recursive jkind computation, prompting [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) to outline a future unification-based approach using layout variables.
- [Leo White](https://github.com/lpw25) similarly questioned whether the topological ordering in [#6177](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6177) implies the recursive case is unresolved, with [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) noting cycles are already forbidden by the unboxed recursion check.
repositories.trends_summary: The week showed concentrated effort on tightening `any`-kind handling in the type system and advancing the 5.4 upstream merge, while flambda2 optimisation correctness received several targeted fixes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Multiple successive PRs from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6145](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6145), [#6156](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6156), [#6143](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6143), [#6173](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6173)) tightened the `any`-kind story for records and variants, moving variable-representation types behind the `-extension layouts_beta` gate.
- [Jules Jacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) submitted a cluster of ikinds correctness PRs ([#6199](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6199), [#6201](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6201), [#6202](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6202), [#6197](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6197)) fixing GADT projection, recursive payload bounds, and error message quality for the inference-based kind system.
- The upstream 5.4 merge is making concrete progress with [#6179](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6179), [#6196](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6196), [#6198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6198), and [#6208](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6208) all landing or advancing this week.
- Runtime 4 removal ([#6172](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6172)) and a unified `OXCAML_RUNTIME` CPP symbol ([#6203](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6203)) signal a push to clean up the runtime layer ahead of a cleaner release.
