repositories.brief_summary: Busy week adding fallocate, ftruncate, shutdown, socket, renameat, symlinkat bindings and fixing Statx API types.
repositories.new_features_summary: New uring bindings for fallocate, ftruncate, shutdown, socket, renameat, symlinkat; improved FLAGS and Statx APIs.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`fallocate` binding** with a `Fallocate_flags` module exposing `keep_size`, `punch_hole`, `collapse_range`, `zero_range`, `insert_range`, `unshare_range`, and `write_zeroes` flags, enabling async disk space manipulation ([#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149))
- New **`ftruncate` binding** for async file size adjustment via io_uring ([#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149))
- New **`shutdown` binding** providing an async version of `shutdown(2)` accepting `Unix.shutdown_command` ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- New **`socket` binding** for async socket creation, returning the new file descriptor through the completion result ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- New **`renameat` binding** with a `Rename_flags` module exposing `noreplace`, `exchange`, and `whiteout` flags for atomic renames and overlay-filesystem use ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- New **`symlinkat` binding** for async symlink creation with optional `dir_fd` for relative paths ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- **`io_uring_submit` errors** are now raised as exceptions instead of returning a raw negative integer, improving error propagation ([#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144))
- **`Statx.Attr` API** overhauled: the inverted `check` function is replaced by a correctly named `mem_checked ~mask` that takes typed `Attr.t` arguments; `attributes` and `attributes_mask` accessors now return `Attr.t` instead of `Int64.t` ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **`Statx.mask` accessor** now returns a typed `Mask.t` value instead of `Int64.t` ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **`FLAGS` module type** extended with `to_int` and a polymorphic `( = )` operator, and the `type t` is now abstract rather than `private int` ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **`Region.init`** simplified to calculate the number of slots automatically from the buffer size and slot size, removing the need to pass a count separately ([#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145))
- **`AT_FDCWD` handling** moved to C stubs, eliminating the use of `Obj.magic` in the OCaml layer ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128))
repositories.activity_summary: Three PRs remain open including an errno module, a bytes-based buffer redesign, and socket-flag support; eight PRs merged.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened** this week and still open: an **errno module** adding Linux-specific error codes by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152)); a **draft PR switching the buffer type from bigarray/cstruct to `bytes`** (surprisingly small diff) ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)); and a PR to **allow setting `CLOEXEC` on socket creation**, motivated by [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843), with discussion about whether this should be always-on ([#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151))
- **Filesystem operations** expanded: `fallocate` and `ftruncate` bindings added with kernel-version guards in tests for the very new `FALLOC_FL_WRITE_ZEROES` flag ([#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149)); `renameat` with rename flags hard-coded for older musl compatibility ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147)); `symlinkat` with corrected symlink-follow test ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- **Networking operations** expanded with `shutdown` and async `socket` creation bindings, reusing the OCaml runtime's socket table rather than redefining it ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- **Null CQE safety check** added to `caml_uring_wait_cqe` as a C `assert`, matching the existing check in the timeout variant ([#148](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/148))
- **Debug-mode build warnings** fixed: corrected `%p` format specifiers and errno sign in `ocaml_uring_unregister_buffers`, and additional `URING_DEBUG` format warnings across `uring_stubs.c` ([#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146))
- **Minimum OCaml version** raised to 5.2, removing a now-unnecessary compiler warning ([#150](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/150))
- **`lintcstubs-arity` depopt** added to the test dependency to ensure `primitives.h` regeneration is caught in CI ([#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143))
repositories.discussions_summary: Design debate on CLOEXEC socket flag defaulting and Statx mask semantics; Region.init API trade-off around underfilling buffers.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Discussion on **whether `CLOEXEC` should be opt-in or always-on** for socket creation: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggested always setting it for safety, while [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) argued uring bindings should follow kernel defaults and that Eio can apply the flag at a higher level ([#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151))
- **`Statx.Attr` semantics** clarified during review of the inverted mask fix: discussion covered the distinction between `stx_mask` (fields filled) and `stx_attributes_mask` (supported attribute flags), and what it means for an attribute to be simultaneously unsupported and set ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **`Region.init` API design**: trade-off between allowing underfilled buffers vs. deriving slot count from buffer size, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggesting the simpler derived approach and noting that `Uring.Region` may eventually move into Eio entirely ([#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145))
- **`caml_uring_wait_cqe` null check**: debate on whether a C `assert` or a runtime OCaml check was more appropriate for a condition that should never occur in practice ([#148](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/148))
repositories.trends_summary: Systematic expansion of uring bindings to cover more Linux syscalls, with typed APIs replacing raw Int64 for Statx fields.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Typed API surfaces replacing raw integers**: the shift of `Statx.attributes`, `attributes_mask`, and `mask` from `Int64.t` to typed `Attr.t` / `Mask.t`, and making `FLAGS.t` fully abstract, reflects a push toward more compile-time–safe interfaces ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **Systematic syscall coverage**: adding `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, `symlinkat`, `fallocate`, and `ftruncate` in one week suggests a focused effort to bring uring bindings to feature parity with commonly needed Linux operations, likely driven by Eio requirements ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147), [#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149))
- **OxCaml compatibility** is an active concern: comments in [#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142) mention avoiding eta-reduction to satisfy OxCaml's stricter requirements and keeping types abstract for future `local` interactions
- **Buffer type migration**: the draft PR [#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) to replace bigarray/cstruct with plain `bytes` signals a potential simplification of the buffer story
repositories.contributors_summary: Anil Madhavapeddy authored nearly all PRs; Thomas Leonard reviewed and co-authored fixes; David Allsopp contributed AT_FDCWD cleanup.
repositories.contributors:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) authored the new `fallocate`/`ftruncate` ([#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149)), `shutdown`/`socket`/`renameat`/`symlinkat` ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147)), `Statx.Attr` fix ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142)), `Region.init` simplification ([#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145)), null CQE check ([#148](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/148)), debug format fixes ([#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146)), OCaml 5.2 minimum cleanup ([#150](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/150)), and opened the errno module ([#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152)), bytes-buffer draft ([#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153)), and CLOEXEC socket flags ([#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151)) PRs
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) contributed the `io_uring_submit` error-reporting fix ([#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144)), performed reviews across multiple PRs including rebase and hard-coded musl rename flags ([#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147)), and provided detailed technical feedback on `Statx` mask semantics ([#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142)), `Region.init` API design ([#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145)), and null CQE check rationale ([#148](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/148))
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) contributed the `AT_FDCWD` handling refactor moving it to C stubs and eliminating `Obj.magic` ([#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128))

repositories.brief_summary: New Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed module replaces Uring.Region API for fixed-buffer allocation; scheduler simplification and new fsync/truncate ops in progress.
repositories.new_features_summary: New Fixed module for fixed-buffer management in eio_linux, plus ftruncate/fdatasync/fsync-via-uring additions pending merge.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed` module** introduced by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), replacing the `Uring.Region` API with an Eio-managed fixed-buffer abstraction ([#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848)). The new module exposes `Fixed.alloc`, `Fixed.alloc_or_wait`, `Fixed.free`, `Fixed.use`, `Fixed.length`, `Fixed.to_cstruct`, `Fixed.to_bigstring`, and `Fixed.to_string`, giving Eio direct control over fixed-buffer pool management rather than relying on the upstream uring library's `Region` API. This is an API-breaking change for any users of fixed buffers via the low-level interface.
- **`ftruncate`, `fdatasync`, and uring-based `fsync`** operations being added to `eio_linux` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) in the open PR ([#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850)), extending the set of file operations available through the io_uring backend.
repositories.activity_summary: Two PRs opened targeting scheduler simplification and new file ops; three PRs merged covering fixed-buffer API, test formatting, and CI linting.
repositories.activity:
- **Scheduler simplification** work opened by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) in [#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849), moving read/write retry logic out of the scheduler and into fiber-level code — removing the special-cased `Read`/`Write` job types and the `file_offset` type, resulting in a cleaner scheduler with fewer fixed-buffer-specific concerns.
- **`ftruncate`/`fdatasync`/`fsync`** additions pending merge from [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850)), which also requires a trunk version of the uring library.
- **Fixed-buffer API migration** to `Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed` merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848)), updating internal usages in `flow.ml` and refactoring the free-pool and waiter queue logic to live inside Eio rather than the uring library.
- **Test IP address formatting** improved by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#847](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/847)) to avoid encoding issues and switch to `ocaml.org` as the example domain for shorter test output, following up on a prior encoding bug ([#575](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/575)).
- **`lintcstubs-arity`** added as a test dependency by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([#846](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/846)) to catch missing C prototype arities in CI, mirroring the same change made in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143).
- Long-standing **`chown` support** PR by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)) adding `fchownat`-based ownership changes to both `eio_posix` and `eio_linux` remains open with active review discussion.
- Issue [#788](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/788) ("Interrupted system call" on parallel stdout writes) closed as resolved by [#842](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/842) via the adoption of `DEFER_TASKRUN` in the Linux backend.
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on whether to restrict io_uring to newer kernels by default, prompted by ZFS fixed-buffer bug and recurring kernel issues.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) raised the question in issue [#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) of whether `Eio_main` should restrict io_uring to kernel 7+ by default, given the accumulation of kernel bugs encountered — including the ZFS fixed-buffer regression that triggered the issue — rather than requiring users to opt in to the posix backend manually.
- In [#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noted interest in also exposing errno values from uring probing, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) confirming that would be useful but is separate from the current fixed-buffer refactor.
repositories.trends_summary: Ongoing effort to reduce eio_linux's coupling to the ocaml-uring library internals, moving buffer management and retry logic into Eio itself.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **decoupling of eio_linux from ocaml-uring internals** is a clear theme: PR [#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848) moves fixed-buffer region management into Eio, and PR [#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849) removes scheduler-level retry handling that was specific to the uring fixed-buffer path. This mirrors ongoing work in the uring library itself ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143)).
- **Kernel version compatibility** concerns are surfacing more explicitly, with discussion in [#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) about gating io_uring use on kernel version to avoid known bugs, and the open uring-sharing hang ([#801](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/801)) pointing to continued reliability challenges with older kernels.
repositories.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard drove most activity with three merged PRs and two open PRs; Anil Madhavapeddy opened a PR and participated in design discussions.
repositories.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) merged three PRs this week: the `Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed` API refactor ([#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848)), test IP address formatting ([#847](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/847)), and lintcstubs-arity CI addition ([#846](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/846)). Also opened two further PRs for scheduler simplification ([#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849)) and reviewed discussion in [#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848) on uring errno probing.
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened the `ftruncate`/`fdatasync`/fsync PR ([#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850)), reviewed and commented on the Fixed module design in [#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848) and scheduler PR [#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849), and raised the kernel version gating question in [#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845).
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) continued work on the long-running `chown` support PR ([#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)) with updated review discussion.

repositories.brief_summary: meio CLI migrated to cmdliner with proper argument parsing, help text, man pages, and -- flag disambiguation.
repositories.new_features_summary: Cmdliner-based CLI adds --help, --version, man pages, and -- flag disambiguation for monitored executables.
repositories.new_features:
- The `meio` executable now uses **cmdliner** for argument parsing, replacing the previous bare `Sys.argv.(1)` approach; this brings proper `--help` output, `--version` support (resolved from `%%VERSION%%`), and a man page with an examples section ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- **Positional argument syntax** `meio <executable> [args...]` is now properly handled, with the executable and its arguments parsed as separate positional arguments rather than split on spaces ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- **`--` disambiguation** is now supported, allowing `meio -- <executable> --flag1 --flag2=foo ...` for monitoring Eio programs that themselves take flags ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- The `meio` binary dependency on **`eio_main`** was removed (it now uses `cmdliner` + `unix`); `eio_main` is retained in `meio.opam` but annotated as only needed for examples ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- **ocamlformat** bumped from `0.26.0` to `0.29.0`, with cosmetic reformatting applied to `task_tree.ml` ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
repositories.activity_summary: PR #75 migrating the meio CLI to cmdliner was merged by Patrick Ferris.
repositories.activity: - **cmdliner CLI migration** ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75)) opened by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) on 2026-05-28 and merged on 2026-06-04; changed 5 files with 49 additions and 19 deletions across the binary entrypoint, dune build file, and opam metadata
repositories.trends_summary: meio is maturing its CLI tooling, reducing ad-hoc argument handling in favour of established OCaml CLI libraries.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The shift from raw `Sys.argv` parsing to **cmdliner** reflects a push toward conventional OCaml CLI ergonomics, giving `meio` standard `--help`/`--version` behaviour expected by end users ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- Removal of the **`eio_main`** runtime dependency from the main binary (retaining it only for examples) suggests an ongoing effort to keep the tool's dependency footprint minimal ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
repositories.contributors_summary: Patrick Ferris authored and merged the cmdliner CLI migration PR.
repositories.contributors: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) authored [#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75), implementing the cmdliner-based CLI rewrite across `src/bin/meio.ml`, `src/bin/dune`, `meio.opam`, and `.ocamlformat`

group_summaries.activity_summary: dune 3.24 compat fixes, Windows CI upgrade-path testing, liquid-ml/migra/awskit PRs opened; WASI tutorial under review on ocaml.org.

- **liquid-ml 0.1.4** (5 packages: `liquid_interpreter`, `liquid_ml`, `liquid_parser`, `liquid_std`, `liquid_syntax`) — Shopify Liquid template engine in OCaml with whitespace-control and improved time handling, by [Ben Faerber](https://github.com/benfaerber); supersedes the 0.1.3 submission ([ocaml/opam-repository#30004](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30004), [ocaml/opam-repository#30003](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30003))

group_summaries.cross_repository_work: - **dune 3.24.0 alpha releases tracked across both repositories** — [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) submitted dune 3.24.0~alpha2 to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29996)) while alpha1 and alpha2 release notes were simultaneously indexed on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) via the automated platform-releases scraper ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3671](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3671)), reflecting coordinated tracking of the dune release cycle across ecosystem infrastructure
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Lockfile invalidation from `conf-*` mutations** — [Yosef](https://github.com/yosefAlsuhaibani) reported in [ocaml/opam-repository#29985](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29985) that in-place changes to `conf-*` dependency requirements broke existing `opam lock` lockfiles and CI/Windows builds; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) reverted the change ([ocaml/opam-repository#29987](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29987)) and noted that locking virtual/conf packages is not fully supported by opam's design
- **Proposal for `opam-packages-option-static-linking`** — [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) opened [ocaml/opam-repository#29975](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29975) proposing convention packages analogous to `ocaml-options` to signal static linking preference ecosystem-wide; [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), and [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) commented, with [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) drawing on MirageOS unikernel toolchain experience; Raphaël published a proof-of-concept branch and a dune-configurator patch as a demo
- **OCaml WASI article technical corrections** — [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) provided detailed corrections on [ocaml/ocaml.org#3667](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3667) covering Wasm 3.0 standardisation status, `--effects=cps` availability, `exnref`-based exception handling now the default for WASI binaries (removing the need for `--enable exnref`), and updated implementation metrics (5200 lines / 90 files)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **dune 3.24 compatibility cascade** — removal of the `lang coq` stanza and changes to `mdx` test targets in dune 3.24 is requiring upper bounds across coq, rocq-runtime, and mdx packages ([ocaml/opam-repository#29988](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29988), [ocaml/opam-repository#29989](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29989), [ocaml/opam-repository#29993](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29993)), with dune 3.24.0~alpha2 already submitted to opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29996)) and indexed on ocaml.org ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3671](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3671))
- **Windows CI testing infrastructure** — [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) is systematically improving Windows CI reliability with upgrade-path testing and PATH pruning to catch real-world depext regressions ([ocaml/opam-repository#29998](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29998))
- **Lockfile semantics and in-place conf-package mutations** — the conf-pkgconf revert ([ocaml/opam-repository#29987](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29987)) and issue discussion ([ocaml/opam-repository#29985](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29985)) highlight ongoing tension between updating package metadata in-place versus publishing new versions for virtual/conf packages that users lock
- **Event mini-sites on ocaml.org** — the OCaml Workshop 2026 pre-site ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3670](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3670)) establishes a pattern for hosting bespoke event pages directly on ocaml.org when conf.researchr.org is unavailable, using a staged rollout approach (URL-accessible but not yet linked from main navigation)

group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Added `%{git-sha}` rule variable expanding to the short git SHA of the current HEAD commit ([ocaml/dune#14810](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14810), [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: `dune-configurator` gains optional `?static` argument to `Configurator.V1.Pkg_config.get` for retrieving static linking options ([ocaml/dune#14947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14947), [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: `(enabled_if)` in `(executable ...)` and `(install ...)` stanzas now allows any available variable, matching `(library ...)` behaviour ([ocaml/dune#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14860), fixes [ocaml/dune#14789](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14789), [Puneeth Chaganti](https://github.com/punchagan))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Melange rules for multi-mode libraries now only generated when `melc` is available in the switch, avoiding build errors for native-only setups ([ocaml/dune#15021](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15021), [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: Parallel dependency cutoffs now restored concurrently rather than serially in watch mode; the `cutoffs_that_reduce_concurrency_in_watch_mode` knob is removed ([ocaml/dune#15012](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15012), [Andrey Mokhov](https://github.com/snowleopard))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: New `Memo.create_rec`, `Memo.Var.Unit`, `Invalidation.custom`, `Invalidation.to_reason_list`, `on_event` node lifecycle callbacks, and `reset_if_necessary` with non-reproducible error tracking ([ocaml/dune#14960](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14960), [ocaml/dune#14965](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14965), [ocaml/dune#14967](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14967), [ocaml/dune#14987](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14987), [ocaml/dune#14973](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14973), [ocaml/dune#14990](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14990), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)**: `--error-reporting=twice` now prints an `==== Error Summary ====` header before the repeated error list ([ocaml/dune#15016](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15016), fixes [ocaml/dune#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14757), [Yahya Ehsan](https://github.com/yussypu))
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)**: OxCaml `@@zero_alloc` annotations now rendered in generated documentation; new `Zero_alloc` model type with `opt`, `strict`, `arity`, and `custom_error_msg` fields added to `Lang.Value`; HTML, LaTeX, man, and Markdown outputs all updated ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), closes [ocaml/odoc#1418](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1418), [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)**: New `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` OCaml bindings with `format`, `formatToParts`, and `resolvedOptions` methods; typed `options` constructor accepting `localeMatcher`, `numberingSystem`, `style`, and `numeric` properties ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070), [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal))
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: `prefix` and `prefixexec` sections proposed for `.install` files, enabling installation relative to the switch prefix ([ocaml/opam#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938), [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian)) — under review
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)**: `OpamTar` module using the `tar` OCaml library instead of the system `tar` tool proposed in [ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) ([R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou)) — marked ready to merge
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune)** was the most active repository with 215 PRs merged, led by an intensive decomposition of the `Memo` engine and a campaign to improve documentation across 20+ issues
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)** merged two PRs: OxCaml `@@zero_alloc` annotation support ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) and OpenBSD portability fixes ([ocaml/odoc#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1442)); two further PRs opened for `ppx_expect` removal and zero_alloc arity refinement
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam)** had seven open PRs covering API cleanup removing 366 unused exports ([ocaml/opam#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954)), tar library integration ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945)), new `.install` file sections ([ocaml/opam#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938)), and git config scoping ([ocaml/opam#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6955)); no commits reached master
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml)** merged `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` bindings ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070)) and opened a draft documentation migration PR to adopt `odoc`/`wodoc` ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261)); several wasm optimisation PRs remain in progress
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)** had a discussion on integrating `ocamlgrep` structural search via a custom LSP request ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)) with maintainer pushback on protocol fit
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)** merged one fix: Emacs `when-let` obsoletion warning removed for Emacs 31 compatibility ([ocaml/merlin#2076](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2076))
- **[ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind)**, **[ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format)**, and **[ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn)** had no recorded activity this week
group_summaries.activity_summary: dune memo refactoring, docs campaign, and Melange fixes dominated; opam, odoc, and js_of_ocaml each had targeted improvements and active open PRs.
group_summaries.activity:
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Memo engine refactoring**: `Memo` decomposed into `node.ml`, `exec.ml`, `parallel.ml`, `deps_collector.ml`, and `value.ml` with formal interfaces ([ocaml/dune#15029](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15029), [ocaml/dune#15036](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15036), [ocaml/dune#15037](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15037), [ocaml/dune#15039](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15039), [ocaml/dune#15040](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15040), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Memo performance**: packed cached value timestamps into one immediate value; hot-path `Run.is_current` check avoids fiber allocation; single hash-table lookup for dep nodes ([ocaml/dune#14970](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14970), [ocaml/dune#14964](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14964), [ocaml/dune#14962](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14962), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Bug fixes**: `dune-rpc-lwt` channel close EBADF fix ([ocaml/dune#14983](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14983), fixes [ocaml/dune#7624](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/7624)); illumos build via `wait4`/EAGAIN retry ([ocaml/dune#14980](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14980), fixes [ocaml/dune#14182](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14182)); memo cycle-detection edge accumulation after first cycle ([ocaml/dune#14968](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14968)); `dune init project` module name validation ([ocaml/dune#15014](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15014)); Melange Merlin source directory lookup ([ocaml/dune#15053](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15053))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Documentation campaign**: 20+ PRs clarifying `dirs`/`data_only_dirs`, generated library module names, `%{exe:}`/`%{bin:}`, root path variables, package version sources, dev/release profiles, `@all` alias, `@doc-json`, bytecode targets, and more ([ocaml/dune#15042](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15042), [ocaml/dune#15046](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15046), [ocaml/dune#15044](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15044), [ocaml/dune#15024](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15024), [ocaml/dune#15028](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15028), [ocaml/dune#15003](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15003), and others, [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **[ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) — Melange**: Disabled ReScript dialect for Melange 1.0 ([ocaml/dune#15022](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15022)); shared test fixtures; jsoo respects `enabled_if` for archive library resolution ([ocaml/dune#14904](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14904), [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon))
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) — OpenBSD portability**: Replaced GNU `grep --context=<NUM>` with `-C<NUM>` and non-portable `sed \n` with `awk` in cram tests ([ocaml/odoc#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1442), [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV))
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) — Newly opened**: `ppx_expect` removal from test infrastructure — 65 files, 10k additions/9k deletions ([ocaml/odoc#1445](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1445), [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)); zero_alloc arity display refinement — omit arity N when it matches the function's own arity ([ocaml/odoc#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444), [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV))
- **[ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) — Closed**: OCaml 5.5 support issue confirmed resolved by odoc 3.2.1 in opam-repository ([ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)); OxCaml `kind_` definition support tracked as new issue ([ocaml/odoc#1443](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1443), [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w))
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) — API cleanup**: [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio) opened [ocaml/opam#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954) removing 366 unused exported elements (−1856 lines across 84 files) via dead code analysis
- **[ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) — Bug fixes**: Fix `opam init --bare` to skip compiler selection ([ocaml/opam#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6957), fixes [ocaml/opam#5815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5815), [Kayo Tei](https://github.com/tuesdayjz)); conditionally apply opam-specific git config only when not doing a full fetch ([ocaml/opam#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6955), fixes [ocaml/opam#6451](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6451), [Dmitri Volkov](https://github.com/sporkl)); draft fix for filename `startswith` behaviour ([ocaml/opam#6953](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6953), [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) — Compiler enhancements (open PRs)**: esbuild-style cosmetic transforms in compact mode: `!0`/`!1` for booleans, stripped leading zeros, normalised exponents, backtick strings ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2257](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2257), [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)); CSS Font Loading API bindings ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2255](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2255), [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)); Node.js `uncaughtException` handler scoped to standalone programs ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2258](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2258), fixes [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1277](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1277), [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo))
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) — Wasm backend (draft PRs)**: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) has multiple drafts targeting local-variable optimisation ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238)), exception handling ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2098](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2098)), WASI runtime ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831)), static evaluation ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2153](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2153)), and GC-after-parsing memory reduction ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2259](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2259))
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp)**: Automated Nix flake lock update refreshing `merlin5_3` and `nixpkgs` open as [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437)
- **[ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin)**: Emacs `when-let` replaced with `let`+`when` in `merlin--construct-point`, silencing Emacs 31 byte-compiler warning while maintaining Emacs 25.1+ compatibility ([ocaml/merlin#2076](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2076), [Danny Willems](https://github.com/dannywillems))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
