group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **[ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)**: [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) proposed integrating `ocamlgrep` structural OCaml search (backed by `.cmt` typed ASTs) as a custom LSP request, developed at LexiFi under [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb)'s supervision; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) questioned the fit, noting the LSP protocol is designed around specific protocol interactions and comparing the proposal to wrapping `ripgrep` inside ocamllsp
- **[ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)**: [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) and [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) discussed whether `@@zero_alloc arity N` should omit the arity when it equals the function's visible arity; they deferred this to the follow-up [ocaml/odoc#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444) to keep the initial PR focused
- **[ocaml/opam#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938)**: [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) reviewed `prefix`/`prefixexec` `.install` file sections, suggesting use of `Pp.check` and `OpamPathName` constants; [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) responded on conservative path validation trade-offs
- **[ocaml/opam#6955](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6955)**: [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) welcomed first-time contributor [Dmitri Volkov](https://github.com/sporkl) on the git config scoping fix and noted the team is focused on finalising the opam 2.6 alpha release
- **[ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261)**: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) reviewed the documentation migration draft, requesting local preview support and updates to `check_manual_links.sh`; [Vincent Balat](https://github.com/balat) addressed both by adding a `--local` flag to `wodoc build` and repurposing the link-check script to external-only checks
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml feature support in [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc)** is progressing incrementally: `@@zero_alloc` rendering is complete ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)); `kind_` definition support ([ocaml/odoc#1443](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1443)) and zero_alloc arity refinement ([ocaml/odoc#1444](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1444)) are next in the OxCaml tracking issue [ocaml/odoc#1411](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1411)
- **Dune `Memo` modularisation**: the incremental computation engine is being decomposed into focused sub-modules (`node.ml`, `exec.ml`, `parallel.ml`, `deps_collector.ml`, `value.ml`) with explicit interfaces ([ocaml/dune#15029](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15029), [ocaml/dune#15036](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15036), [ocaml/dune#15039](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15039)), signalling intent to extend and reason about the engine more formally
- **opam 2.6 alpha preparation**: large-scale API cleanup removing 366 unused exports ([ocaml/opam#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954)), replacement of system `tar` tool with the OCaml `tar` library ([ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945)), and new `.install` file sections ([ocaml/opam#6938](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6938)) collectively reflect a quality and portability push ahead of the alpha milestone
- **Documentation toolchain modernisation**: dune's documentation campaign ([ocaml/dune#15042](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15042) and 20+ others) closed many issues dating to 2020–2022; js_of_ocaml is migrating from a custom wiki/Python pipeline to `odoc`+`wodoc` ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2261](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2261)), aligning with OCaml toolchain conventions
- **Cross-platform portability focus**: OpenBSD grep/awk fixes in odoc ([ocaml/odoc#1442](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1442)) and illumos `wait4`/EAGAIN fixes in dune ([ocaml/dune#14980](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14980)) continue a recurring pattern of expanding platform coverage across the toolchain
- **LSP server scope**: the ocamlgrep integration proposal ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607)) and maintainer response illustrate a recurring question about whether whole-project analysis tools belong inside the LSP server or remain independent, with a stated preference for keeping ocaml-lsp focused on the LSP protocol
- **Wasm backend investment**: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) has five concurrent draft PRs targeting compilation optimisation, exception handling, WASI support, and peak memory reduction in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2098](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2098), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1831](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1831), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2153](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2153), [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2259](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2259))

group_summaries.brief_summary: oxcaml/oxcaml saw dense typing and flambda2 fixes plus a 5.4 upstream merge push; oxcaml/merlin advanced its minus-37 branch merge.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Match-in-match heuristic, normal-form mode morphisms, frametables in .rodata, fexpr expect tests, and OXCAML_RUNTIME CPP symbol landed in oxcaml/oxcaml.
group_summaries.new_features:
- The **match-in-match heuristic** landed in flambda2 via [oxcaml/oxcaml#4374](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4374) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp), enabling continuation specialisation across nested match expressions and exposed via a new `-flambda2-match-in-match` flag with a `cont_spec_threshold` parameter.
- **Normal-form mode morphisms** merged in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5453](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5453), refactoring the mode solver's internal representation to enable more precise mode error hints via concrete modality propagation.
- Frametables are now placed in the **`.rodata` section** by default via [oxcaml/oxcaml#6094](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6094) by [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski), separating instruction and data pages to improve iTLB efficiency.
- **`%%expect_fexpr`** annotations can now appear in toplevel expect tests via [oxcaml/oxcaml#5837](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5837) by [Keryan Didier](https://github.com/Keryan-dev), allowing raw, simplified, and reaper-stage flambda2 IR to be snapshot-tested inline.
- A new **`OXCAML_RUNTIME`** C preprocessor symbol was added in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6203](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6203) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes), giving external stubs a single canonical way to detect an OxCaml runtime.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin); [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository), [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml), and [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) had no recorded activity.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) shipped five notable features including the match-in-match heuristic ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4374](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4374)) and frametable relocation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6094](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6094)), alongside a wave of `any`-kind typing correctness fixes and the 5.2.0minus-40 magic number bump ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6204](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6204)).
- [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) has a large draft PR ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) merging minus-37 changes into the 5.4 branch (896 files changed), currently blocked on conflict resolution but receiving positive review feedback.
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dense release day on 2026-06-05 with the minus-40 magic bump, multiple typing and flambda2 fixes, active 5.4 upstream merge work, and a large Merlin branch merge in progress.
group_summaries.activity:
- [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) bumped the 5.2.0minus-40 magic number in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6204](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6204) and reverted the cmx-skipping optimisation [oxcaml/oxcaml#6065](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6065) via [oxcaml/oxcaml#6206](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6206) pending more tests.
- [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) merged a cluster of `any`-kind correctness fixes: topological jkind ordering ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6177](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6177)), its follow-up ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6209](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6209)), sort variables for `any`-kinded fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6145](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6145)), and gating variable-representation types behind `-extension layouts_beta` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6173](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6173)), plus atomic field and record representation fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6131](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6131), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6143](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6143), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6156](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6156)).
- [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) merged a static-constants code-size accounting fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5992](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5992)) and dropped spuriously created variables during nested env-extension joins ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6170](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6170)).
- [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) merged the 5.4 Merlin update ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6198)) and immediately fixed a parser conflict regression it introduced ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6210](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6210)).
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) merged a 5.4 sub-message location fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6208](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6208)) and a CR cleanup ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6196](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6196)) for the upstream merge.
- [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan) fixed a `Gc.quick_stat` bug in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6164](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6164).
- [George Shammas](https://github.com/georgyo) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#6213](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6213) updating nixpkgs to 26.05 with an LLVM 21 build fix.
- [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#6182](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6182) adding `-remap` and `-alias` flags for redirecting persistent module references.
- [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) opened draft [oxcaml/oxcaml#6158](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6158) instrumenting the compiler to trace variable UID availability, showing ~57% of typed variables currently reach debug info.
- [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) has a draft [oxcaml/oxcaml#6172](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6172) in progress to remove runtime 4 code from the tree.
- [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) opened [oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239) to merge minus-37 changes into the merlin 5.4 branch (896 files, +298180/-51224 lines), currently marked conflicting.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- Merlin integration is active across both repos: [oxcaml/oxcaml#6211](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6211) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) merges 5.2.0minus-40 compiler changes into the Merlin subtree, while [oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239) merges minus-37 changes into the standalone Merlin 5.4 branch.
- [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#6207](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6207) to install `eval.mli` into the compiler install directory, directly unblocking Merlin's jump-to-definition support for that library.
- The 5.4 upstream merge PRs in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6179](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6179), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6196](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6196), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6198), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6208](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6208)) form a coordinated sequence, with each building on upstream OCaml changes that also flow into the Merlin merge in [oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239).
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) opened [oxcaml/oxcaml#6180](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6180) noting that flambda2 eagerly unboxes variant fields even when no local allocation is present; the proposed fix is to only unbox fields that already have at least one use with the field available.
- [oxcaml/oxcaml#6146](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6146) (closed) discussed the recommended idiom for pairing `@ unique` destroy with interleaved read operations on C handles; the thread concluded that with-resource combinators or consume-and-return threading are current options and borrowing is not yet available.
- The **box types** PR [oxcaml/oxcaml#5342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5342) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) saw continued review with [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) near-approving, with open questions around GADT exhaustiveness and `mcomp` aliasability for `_ box`.
- [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) raised a concern in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6177](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6177) about topological ordering as a long-term solution to recursive jkind computation; [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) outlined a future unification-based approach using layout variables.
- [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) reviewed the key files in [oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239) and found the minus-37 merge changes reasonable, providing a positive signal for the large-scale integration.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- Multiple successive PRs from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6145](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6145), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6156](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6156), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6143](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6143), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6173](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6173)) are tightening the `any`-kind story for records and variants, progressively moving variable-representation types behind the `-extension layouts_beta` gate.
- [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) submitted a cluster of ikinds correctness PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6199](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6199), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6201](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6201), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6202](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6202), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6197](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6197)) fixing GADT projection, recursive payload bounds, and error message quality.
- The OCaml 5.4 upstream merge is making concrete progress with multiple PRs landing in sequence ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6179](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6179), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6196](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6196), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6198), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6208](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6208)).
- Runtime 4 removal ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6172](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6172)) and a unified `OXCAML_RUNTIME` CPP symbol ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6203](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6203)) signal a push to clean up the runtime layer ahead of a cleaner release.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Bug fixes for runtime events, flambda, type checker, and build system; RFC #52 implicit source positions design resolved.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: DragonFly BSD frame pointer support, AR/ARFLAGS build standardization, runtime_events hang fix, flambda stub inlining fix.
group_summaries.new_features:
- Fixed infinite loop in `runtime_events.c`'s `write_to_ring` when events exceed the ring buffer size — runtime now terminates with a fatal error rather than hanging ([ocaml/ocaml#14834](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14834), fixes [ocaml/ocaml#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13335))
- Fixed warning 60 (unused-module) source location for non-aliasable module substitutions — now points to the substitution site rather than the original declaration ([ocaml/ocaml#14831](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14831), fixes [ocaml/ocaml#12745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12745))
- Fixed infinite recursive stub inlining in flambda when optional argument defaults reference the enclosing function ([ocaml/ocaml#14839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14839), fixes [ocaml/ocaml#14828](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14828))
- Added DragonFly BSD frame pointer support for AMD64, resolving assembler errors with gcc 8.3 and binutils 2.34 ([ocaml/ocaml#14840](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14840))
- `AR` and `ARFLAGS` are now exported in `Makefile.config` and support configure-time override; `ARCMD` deprecated as of OCaml 5.5 ([ocaml/ocaml#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552))
- Fixed type recovery error messages: paths are now deep-copied when freezing type expressions, preventing later unification from changing module names in earlier errors ([ocaml/ocaml#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824))
- Fixed `configure.ac` incorrect `break` statements that broke default-argument detection, resolving broken installation on OpenSUSE ([ocaml/ocaml#14846](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14846))
- Corrected singular/plural grammar in warning 57 message, upstreamed from OxCaml ([ocaml/ocaml#14843](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14843))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) merged 8 fixes across runtime, type system, flambda, platform support, and build system; 2 new PRs opened for stdlib additions and a typechecker crash fix
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) saw activity on [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) (implicit source positions) following a developer meeting that reached design consensus
- Nine contributors merged or opened code in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) this week, spanning runtime, type system, build system, platform support, and stdlib
- Developer meeting outcomes drove activity in both repositories: design decisions on implicit source positions ([ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)), AST extension points ([ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668)), and extensible IO channels ([ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775))
- Two new contributors merged their first patches: [Ryan Stewart](https://github.com/RyanJamesStewart) fixed a long-standing warning location bug ([ocaml/ocaml#14831](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14831)) and [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) improved testsuite error messages ([ocaml/ocaml#14844](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14844))
group_summaries.activity_summary: New stdlib sort proposals for Dynarray, active multi-domain GC and type system work, and C API extension proposals under review.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Newly opened PRs**: [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) proposed `Dynarray.sort`, `Dynarray.stable_sort`, and `Dynarray.stable_sort_sub` stdlib additions with benchmarks ([ocaml/ocaml#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848)); [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) opened a fix for the typechecker crash (`Fatal error: exception Ctype.Unify(_)`) reported in [ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842) ([ocaml/ocaml#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845))
- **Runtime system**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has an open PR improving multi-domain ephemeron marking to avoid redundant rounds ([ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774)) with benchmarks showing ~14% speedup at 12 domains
- **Type system**: [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) merged the type recovery path-copying fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824)) and backported a related fix to the 5.5 branch ([ocaml/ocaml#14841](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14841)); [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) are actively debugging the `Ctype.Unify(_)` regression ([ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842))
- **Compiler optimizations**: [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) has an open PR to merge nested labelled applications in Lambda, eliminating spurious intermediate closures ([ocaml/ocaml#14763](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14763)); [Zane Hambly](https://github.com/Zaneham) continues review on the multi-architecture `Iatomic_fetch_add` native codegen PR ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)) with [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) reviewing amd64, arm64, riscv, and power backends
- **C API**: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) has an open proposal adding `caml_result_find_exception` to `mlvalues.h` for safer exception inspection from C ([ocaml/ocaml#14814](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14814))
- **GC performance**: [ocaml/ocaml#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733) (Goblint 4.14 vs 5.x regression) received updated benchmarks showing a +9% CPU regression on 5.5 (down from +14% earlier), with GC shortcut optimization partially closing the gap
- **RFCs**: [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) posted developer meeting outcomes on the implicit source positions RFC ([ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)), with plans to update the upstream implementation in [ocaml/ocaml#13886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13886)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- The **implicit source positions** RFC ([ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) is directly linked to the upstream OCaml implementation PR ([ocaml/ocaml#13886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13886)); developer meeting consensus on using a single abstract location type will now drive updates to both
- Developer meeting discussions spanned both repositories: the AST extension points proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668)) and extensible IO channels ([ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775)) in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) were both resolved or advanced by the same meeting that addressed [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)
- OxCaml-to-upstream porting continued with fixes originating in the OxCaml fork landing in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml): warning 57 grammar correction ([ocaml/ocaml#14843](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14843)) and flambda stub inlining fix ([ocaml/ocaml#14839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14839))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) updated [ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668) on adding extension points to the AST to improve ppxlib's AST freeze strategy; [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) summarized developer meeting consensus to include an `extension` case for all syntactic categories without surface syntax initially
- [ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842) reports a typechecker regression (`Fatal error: exception Ctype.Unify(_)`) on trunk affecting downstream packages including dolmen and containers; [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) are debugging level-escape and abbreviation-expansion issues in modular explicit types, with fix under review in [ocaml/ocaml#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845)
- Discussion in [ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842) included [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) raising concerns about LLM-generated text in bug reports diluting useful diagnostic information, with a suggestion to add guidance to `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) (extensible IO channels by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube)) gathered developer meeting feedback on seekable channel handling, stdlib API surface, js_of_ocaml compatibility, and C FFI backward compatibility; soft consensus to allow extensible IO channels
- The implicit source positions RFC ([ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) reached design consensus at the developer meeting: a single abstract `location` type is acceptable, with [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere)'s preferred alternative (option "1a + 2x") not represented due to absence; updates planned for [ocaml/ocaml#13886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13886)
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml upstream contributions** continue with warning grammar corrections ([ocaml/ocaml#14843](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14843)) and flambda fixes ([ocaml/ocaml#14839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14839)) originating in the OxCaml fork finding their way into trunk
- **Modular explicit types** remain an active correctness area — multiple issues and PRs this week ([ocaml/ocaml#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824), [ocaml/ocaml#14841](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14841), [ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842), [ocaml/ocaml#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845)) address edge cases in type-level path handling and functor argument scoping
- **Multi-domain GC tuning** continues across multiple PRs: ephemeron marking optimization ([ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774)), Goblint performance investigation ([ocaml/ocaml#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733)), and mark-delay work ([ocaml/ocaml#13580](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13580)) all reflect sustained effort to close the performance gap with OCaml 4.x on GC-heavy workloads
- **New contributors** are actively merging patches: [Ryan Stewart](https://github.com/RyanJamesStewart) fixed a long-standing warning location bug ([ocaml/ocaml#14831](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14831)) and [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) contributed both a testsuite improvement ([ocaml/ocaml#14844](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14844)) and a new stdlib proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848)) this week
- **Developer meeting outcomes** are increasingly driving RFC and implementation work in tandem, with [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52), [ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668), and [ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) all advancing based on meeting consensus this week

group_summaries.brief_summary: ocaml-uring adds six syscall bindings; eio migrates fixed-buffer management to Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed; meio CLI migrated to cmdliner.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New uring bindings for six syscalls, Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed module, and cmdliner-based meio CLI with --help/--version support.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **[ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio)**: New `Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed` module introduced by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), replacing the `Uring.Region` API with an Eio-managed fixed-buffer abstraction ([ocaml-multicore/eio#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848)); exposes `Fixed.alloc`, `Fixed.alloc_or_wait`, `Fixed.free`, `Fixed.use`, `Fixed.length`, `Fixed.to_cstruct`, `Fixed.to_bigstring`, and `Fixed.to_string`
- **[ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio)**: `ftruncate`, `fdatasync`, and uring-based `fsync` operations being added to `eio_linux` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850)), extending the file operations available through the io_uring backend
- **[ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring)**: New `fallocate` binding with a `Fallocate_flags` module (`keep_size`, `punch_hole`, `collapse_range`, `zero_range`, `insert_range`, `unshare_range`, `write_zeroes`) and `ftruncate` binding for async file size adjustment ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149))
- **[ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring)**: New `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat` (with `Rename_flags`: `noreplace`, `exchange`, `whiteout`), and `symlinkat` bindings for async networking and filesystem operations ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- **[ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring)**: `Statx.Attr` API overhauled — inverted `check` function replaced by `mem_checked ~mask`; `attributes` and `attributes_mask` now return typed `Attr.t` instead of `Int64.t`; `Statx.mask` returns typed `Mask.t`; `FLAGS.t` is now abstract ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- **[ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring)**: `io_uring_submit` errors now raised as exceptions instead of returning raw negative integers ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144))
- **[ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio)**: The `meio` executable now uses cmdliner for argument parsing, adding `--help`, `--version`, man pages with examples, proper positional argument handling, and `--` flag disambiguation ([ocaml-multicore/meio#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity was concentrated in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring), and [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio); the remaining six repositories (domainslib, saturn, eio-trace, picos, kcas, ocaml-iomux) had no activity this week
- [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) was the most active repo: eight PRs merged and three open, adding six new syscall bindings (`fallocate`, `ftruncate`, `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, `symlinkat`) and overhauling the `Statx` typed API
- [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) merged three PRs and opened two more, with work focused on decoupling fixed-buffer management from the uring library and adding new file operations
- [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio) completed a CLI migration to cmdliner, improving the user-facing interface of the Eio monitoring tool
- Key contributors: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), and [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)
group_summaries.activity_summary: Scheduler simplification in eio, typed Statx APIs in ocaml-uring, and lintcstubs-arity CI checks added to both eio and ocaml-uring.
group_summaries.activity:
- **eio scheduler simplification**: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849), moving read/write retry logic out of the scheduler into fiber-level code, removing the special-cased `Read`/`Write` job types and the `file_offset` type
- **eio chown support**: Long-running PR by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#781](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/781)) adding `fchownat`-based ownership changes to both `eio_posix` and `eio_linux` remains open with active review
- **eio test improvements**: IP address formatting in tests improved by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#847](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/847)) to avoid encoding issues, switching to `ocaml.org` as the example domain
- **ocaml-uring null CQE safety**: A C `assert` added to `caml_uring_wait_cqe` matching the existing check in the timeout variant ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#148](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/148))
- **ocaml-uring debug build fixes**: Corrected `%p` format specifiers and errno sign in `ocaml_uring_unregister_buffers` ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146))
- **ocaml-uring minimum OCaml version**: Raised to 5.2, removing an unnecessary compiler warning ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#150](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/150))
- **ocaml-uring Region.init simplification**: Slot count now derived automatically from buffer size and slot size, removing the need to pass a separate count ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145))
- **ocaml-uring AT_FDCWD cleanup**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) moved `AT_FDCWD` handling to C stubs, eliminating use of `Obj.magic` in the OCaml layer ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#128](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/128))
- **lintcstubs-arity CI**: Added as a test dependency in both [ocaml-multicore/eio#846](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/846) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143) to catch missing C prototype arities
- **ocaml-uring errno module**: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) opened [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#152](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/152) adding Linux-specific error codes
- **ocaml-uring bytes-buffer draft**: Draft PR [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) exploring switching the buffer type from bigarray/cstruct to plain `bytes`
- **ocaml-uring CLOEXEC socket flag**: PR [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151) to allow setting `CLOEXEC` on socket creation, motivated by [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843)
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Fixed-buffer decoupling (eio ↔ ocaml-uring)**: [ocaml-multicore/eio#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848) introduces `Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed` to move fixed-buffer pool management out of the uring library and into Eio itself; [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145) simplifies `Region.init` in parallel, with discussion noting `Uring.Region` may eventually move into Eio entirely
- **ftruncate/fdatasync in eio requires ocaml-uring trunk**: [ocaml-multicore/eio#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850) (new file ops) depends on the `ftruncate` binding added in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149), creating a direct dependency on unreleased uring work
- **lintcstubs-arity CI coordination**: The same `lintcstubs-arity` test dependency was added to both [ocaml-multicore/eio#846](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/846) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#143](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/143), standardising C stub arity checking across both repos
- **CLOEXEC socket flag motivated by eio**: [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151) was opened specifically in response to [ocaml-multicore/eio#843](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/843), showing eio requirements driving uring API design
- **Kernel version compatibility shared concern**: [ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) (discussion on gating io_uring to kernel 7+) reflects issues that affect both eio and its uring backend, with kernel bugs surfacing across both repositories
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Kernel version gating for io_uring**: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) raised in [ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) whether `Eio_main` should restrict io_uring to kernel 7+ by default, given accumulating kernel bugs including a ZFS fixed-buffer regression
- **CLOEXEC socket flag defaulting**: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) argued for always setting `CLOEXEC` for safety; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) argued uring bindings should follow kernel defaults and Eio can apply the flag at a higher level ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151))
- **Statx mask semantics**: Discussion in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142) clarified the distinction between `stx_mask` (fields filled) and `stx_attributes_mask` (supported attribute flags), and the meaning of an attribute being simultaneously unsupported and set
- **Region.init API design**: Trade-off between allowing underfilled buffers vs. deriving slot count from buffer size, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggesting the simpler derived approach in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145)
- **Uring errno probing**: In [ocaml-multicore/eio#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noted interest in exposing errno values from uring probing, with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) confirming utility but deferring to a separate PR
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Decoupling eio_linux from ocaml-uring internals**: [ocaml-multicore/eio#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848) moves fixed-buffer region management into Eio, and [ocaml-multicore/eio#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849) removes scheduler-level retry handling specific to the uring fixed-buffer path; this mirrors simplification work in the uring library itself ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145))
- **Typed APIs replacing raw integers in ocaml-uring**: Shift of `Statx.attributes`, `attributes_mask`, and `mask` from `Int64.t` to typed `Attr.t`/`Mask.t`, and making `FLAGS.t` fully abstract ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142)), reflects a push toward compile-time–safe interfaces
- **Systematic Linux syscall coverage**: Adding `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, `symlinkat`, `fallocate`, and `ftruncate` in a single week ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149)) suggests a focused effort to bring uring bindings to feature parity with commonly needed Linux operations, likely driven by Eio requirements
- **OxCaml compatibility as a design constraint**: Comments in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142) note avoiding eta-reduction to satisfy OxCaml's stricter requirements and keeping types abstract for future `local` interactions
- **Buffer type simplification under consideration**: Draft PR [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#153](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/153) to replace bigarray/cstruct with plain `bytes` signals a potential reduction in the buffer abstraction stack
- **Kernel version compatibility as an explicit concern**: Discussion in [ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) and the open hang bug [ocaml-multicore/eio#801](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/801) indicate growing consideration of gating io_uring features on kernel version

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Dune 3.24.0 alpha1/alpha2 released; OxCaml minus-40 bump and typing fixes; uring gains six syscalls; OCaml Workshop 2026 site launched.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: Dune adds %{git-sha}, pkg-config --static; odoc renders @@zero_alloc; uring gains six syscalls; OCaml Workshop 2026 mini-site live on ocaml.org.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed infinite loop in **`runtime_events`** `write_to_ring` when events exceed ring buffer size — runtime now terminates with a fatal error ([ocaml/ocaml#14834](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14834), fixes [ocaml/ocaml#13335](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13335))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed warning 60 (unused-module) **source location** for non-aliasable module substitutions — now points to substitution site rather than original declaration ([ocaml/ocaml#14831](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14831), fixes [ocaml/ocaml#12745](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/12745))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed infinite recursive **stub inlining in flambda** when optional argument defaults reference the enclosing function ([ocaml/ocaml#14839](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14839), fixes [ocaml/ocaml#14828](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14828))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Added **DragonFly BSD frame pointer** support for AMD64, resolving assembler errors with gcc 8.3 and binutils 2.34 ([ocaml/ocaml#14840](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14840))
- __RUMINANT:core__ `AR` and `ARFLAGS` now exported in `Makefile.config` with configure-time override support; **`ARCMD` deprecated** as of OCaml 5.5 ([ocaml/ocaml#14552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14552))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed **type recovery error messages**: paths are now deep-copied when freezing type expressions, preventing later unification from changing module names in earlier errors ([ocaml/ocaml#14824](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14824))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Corrected **singular/plural grammar** in warning 57 message, upstreamed from OxCaml ([ocaml/ocaml#14843](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14843))
- __RUMINANT:core__ Fixed `configure.ac` incorrect `break` statements that broke **default-argument detection**, resolving broken installation on OpenSUSE ([ocaml/ocaml#14846](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14846))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Match-in-match heuristic** landed in flambda2 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4374](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4374)), enabling continuation specialisation across nested match expressions via new `-flambda2-match-in-match` flag
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Normal-form mode morphisms** merged ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5453](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5453)), refactoring the mode solver's internal representation for more precise mode error hints via concrete modality propagation
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ Frametables now placed in **`.rodata` section** by default ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6094](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6094)), separating instruction and data pages to improve iTLB efficiency
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`%%expect_fexpr`** annotations now available in toplevel expect tests ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5837](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5837)), allowing flambda2 IR snapshot testing inline
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ New **`OXCAML_RUNTIME`** C preprocessor symbol added ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6203](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6203)) by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes), giving external stubs a canonical way to detect an OxCaml runtime
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ New `Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed` module introduced by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), replacing `Uring.Region` API with an **Eio-managed fixed-buffer abstraction** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ Six new **io_uring syscall bindings** in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring): `fallocate`, `ftruncate`, `shutdown`, `socket`, `renameat`, and `symlinkat` ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#149](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/149), [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#147](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/147))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`Statx.Attr` API overhauled** — inverted `check` replaced by `mem_checked ~mask`; typed `Attr.t`/`Mask.t` return types replace `Int64.t`; `FLAGS.t` made abstract ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ `io_uring_submit` errors now **raised as exceptions** instead of returning raw negative integers ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`meio` CLI migrated to cmdliner** with `--help`, `--version`, man pages with examples, and proper positional argument handling ([ocaml-multicore/meio#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`%{git-sha}` rule variable** added to dune, expanding to the short git SHA of current HEAD ([ocaml/dune#14810](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14810))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ `dune-configurator` gains optional `?static` argument to `Configurator.V1.Pkg_config.get` for **static linking options** ([ocaml/dune#14947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14947), [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`(enabled_if)` in `(executable)` and `(install)` stanzas** now allows any available variable, matching `(library)` behaviour ([ocaml/dune#14860](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14860), fixes [ocaml/dune#14789](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14789))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Melange rules** for multi-mode libraries now only generated when `melc` is available in the switch, avoiding build errors for native-only setups ([ocaml/dune#15021](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15021))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ Parallel dependency **cutoffs restored concurrently** in watch mode; `cutoffs_that_reduce_concurrency_in_watch_mode` knob removed ([ocaml/dune#15012](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15012))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ New **`Memo` public API** additions: `Memo.create_rec`, `Memo.Var.Unit`, `Invalidation.custom`, `on_event` node lifecycle callbacks, and `reset_if_necessary` with non-reproducible error tracking ([ocaml/dune#14960](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14960), [ocaml/dune#14965](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14965), [ocaml/dune#14967](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14967), [ocaml/dune#14987](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14987))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`--error-reporting=twice`** now prints an `==== Error Summary ====` header before repeated error list ([ocaml/dune#15016](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15016), fixes [ocaml/dune#14757](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14757))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ OxCaml **`@@zero_alloc` annotations** now rendered in generated odoc documentation; new `Zero_alloc` model type with `opt`, `strict`, `arity`, and `custom_error_msg` fields ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), closes [ocaml/odoc#1418](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1418))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ New **`Intl.RelativeTimeFormat`** OCaml bindings in js_of_ocaml with `format`, `formatToParts`, and `resolvedOptions` methods ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **OCaml Workshop 2026 mini-site** launched on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) with three new routes (`/ocaml-workshop-2026`, `/ocaml-workshop-2026/cfp`, `/ocaml-workshop-2026/committee`) featuring tab navigation and a "Submit a Talk" CTA ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3670](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3670))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **raylib 2.0.0** — OCaml bindings for the Raylib game framework published by [Tobias Mock](https://github.com/tjammer) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29978](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29978))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **catala 1.2.0** — law-as-code language and LSP server update by [vbot](https://github.com/vincent-botbol) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29983](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29983))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **awso 0.9.1** — seven-package AWS OCaml SDK by [Michael Bacarella](https://github.com/mbacarella) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29967](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29967))
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **awa 0.6.1** — SSH library fixing `MSG_KEX_n` and `MSG_USERAUTH_n` parsing by [Hannes Mehnert](https://github.com/hannesm) ([ocaml/opam-repository#30001](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/30001))

multicore: -New `Eio_linux.Low_level.Fixed` module introduced by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), replacing `Uring.Region` API with an **Eio-managed fixed-buffer abstraction** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#848](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/848))

-**`Statx.Attr` API overhauled** — inverted `check` replaced by `mem_checked ~mask`; typed `Attr.t`/`Mask.t` return types replace `Int64.t`; `FLAGS.t` made abstract ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#142](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/142))
-`io_uring_submit` errors now **raised as exceptions** instead of returning raw negative integers ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#144](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/144))

-**`meio` CLI migrated to cmdliner** with `--help`, `--version`, man pages with examples, and proper positional argument handling ([ocaml-multicore/meio#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) merged 8 fixes spanning runtime events, type system, flambda, platform support, and build tooling; 9 contributors active; RFC [ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52) (implicit source positions) reached design consensus at a developer meeting; two new contributors — [Ryan Stewart](https://github.com/RyanJamesStewart) and [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) — merged their first patches
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) had a dense week: the **minus-40 magic number bump** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6204](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6204)), five new features including the **match-in-match heuristic** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4374](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/4374)) and **frametable relocation to `.rodata`** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6094](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6094)), and a wave of `any`-kind typing correctness fixes from [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa); [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) has a large 896-file draft PR ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) merging minus-37 changes into the 5.4 branch
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) was the most active multicore repo with 8 PRs merged and 3 open, adding six syscall bindings and overhauling the `Statx` typed API; [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) merged 3 PRs decoupling fixed-buffer management from the uring library; [ocaml-multicore/meio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio) completed its CLI migration to cmdliner
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) merged 215 PRs this week led by an intensive decomposition of the **`Memo` engine** into formal sub-modules and a 20+ PR documentation campaign; Dune 3.24.0 alpha1 and alpha2 were released; [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) added OxCaml `@@zero_alloc` rendering; [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) is advancing toward a 2.6 alpha with API cleanup and tar library integration
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) merged 10+ package releases; dune 3.24 compatibility caused `dune < 3.24` upper bounds to propagate across coq, rocq, and mdx packages; [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) launched the OCaml Workshop 2026 pre-event mini-site; Windows CI gained upgrade-path testing from [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)

weekly_summary.activity_summary: Memo engine refactored in dune; multi-domain GC and type system work in OCaml; OxCaml 5.4 upstream merge progressing; uring buffer APIs simplified.
weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Dynarray sort proposals**: [Jeannalyse](https://github.com/Jeannalyse) proposed `Dynarray.sort`, `Dynarray.stable_sort`, and `Dynarray.stable_sort_sub` stdlib additions with benchmarks ([ocaml/ocaml#14848](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14848))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Typechecker regression** (`Fatal error: exception Ctype.Unify(_)`) on trunk affecting dolmen and containers being debugged by [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) ([ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842)), with fix under review in [ocaml/ocaml#14845](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14845)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Multi-domain ephemeron marking** optimization by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) — open PR to avoid redundant rounds ([ocaml/ocaml#14774](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14774)) with ~14% speedup at 12 domains; GC performance regression on OCaml 5.5 vs 4.14 (Goblint benchmark [ocaml/ocaml#13733](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13733)) shows +9% CPU regression (improved from +14% earlier)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Multi-architecture `Iatomic_fetch_add`** native codegen PR ([ocaml/ocaml#14575](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14575)) with [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole) reviewing amd64, arm64, riscv, and power backends
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Nested labelled applications** in Lambda — open PR by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) to merge nested applications, eliminating spurious intermediate closures ([ocaml/ocaml#14763](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14763))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`caml_result_find_exception` C API** proposal by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) adding safer exception inspection from C ([ocaml/ocaml#14814](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14814))
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Implicit source positions RFC** ([ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) developer meeting outcome posted by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole); design consensus on a single abstract location type; updates planned for upstream implementation [ocaml/ocaml#13886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13886)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) bumped the **5.2.0minus-40 magic number** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6204](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6204)) and reverted the cmx-skipping optimisation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6206](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6206)) pending more tests
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) merged a cluster of **`any`-kind correctness fixes**: topological jkind ordering ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6177](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6177)), sort variables for `any`-kinded fields ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6145](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6145)), and gating variable-representation types behind `-extension layouts_beta` ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6173](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6173))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) submitted a cluster of **ikinds correctness PRs** fixing GADT projection, recursive payload bounds, and error message quality ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6199](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6199), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6201](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6201), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6202](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6202), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6197](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6197))
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **5.4 upstream merge** progressing through a coordinated sequence of PRs: [oxcaml/oxcaml#6179](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6179), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6196](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6196), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6198), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6208](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6208), with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) handling sub-message location fixes
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Runtime 4 removal** draft by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6172](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6172)); debug variable UID tracing draft by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) showing ~57% of typed variables reach debug info ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6158](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6158))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **eio scheduler simplification**: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849) moving read/write retry logic from scheduler into fiber-level code, removing special-cased `Read`/`Write` job types
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **`ftruncate`, `fdatasync`, and uring-based `fsync`** operations being added to `eio_linux` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850))
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **ocaml-uring housekeeping**: C `assert` added for null CQE safety ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#148](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/148)), debug format specifiers corrected ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146)), minimum OCaml version raised to 5.2 ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#150](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/150)), `Region.init` slot count now auto-derived ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune `Memo` engine decomposed** into `node.ml`, `exec.ml`, `parallel.ml`, `deps_collector.ml`, and `value.ml` with formal interfaces by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) ([ocaml/dune#15029](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15029), [ocaml/dune#15036](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15036), [ocaml/dune#15037](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15037))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune `Memo` performance**: packed cached value timestamps into one immediate value; hot-path `Run.is_current` avoids fiber allocation; single hash-table lookup for dep nodes ([ocaml/dune#14970](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14970), [ocaml/dune#14964](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14964), [ocaml/dune#14962](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14962))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune documentation campaign**: 20+ PRs by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) clarifying `dirs`/`data_only_dirs`, generated library module names, `%{exe:}`/`%{bin:}`, root path variables, package version sources, dev/release profiles, `@all` alias, bytecode targets, and more ([ocaml/dune#15042](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15042), [ocaml/dune#15046](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15046), [ocaml/dune#15044](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15044), [ocaml/dune#15024](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15024))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune bug fixes**: `dune-rpc-lwt` EBADF on channel close ([ocaml/dune#14983](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14983), fixes [ocaml/dune#7624](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/7624)); illumos `wait4`/EAGAIN retry ([ocaml/dune#14980](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14980)); `dune init project` module name validation ([ocaml/dune#15014](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/15014))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam API cleanup**: [Corentin De Souza](https://github.com/fantazio) opened [ocaml/opam#6954](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6954) removing 366 unused exported elements (−1856 lines across 84 files); `OpamTar` module replacing system `tar` tool with the OCaml `tar` library in [ocaml/opam#6945](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6945) marked ready to merge
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam `--bare` fix**: `opam init --bare` corrected to skip compiler selection ([ocaml/opam#6957](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6957), fixes [ocaml/opam#5815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/5815))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Merlin Emacs 31 compatibility**: `when-let` replaced with `let`+`when` in `merlin--construct-point` ([ocaml/merlin#2076](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2076))

-**5.4 upstream merge** progressing through a coordinated sequence of PRs: [oxcaml/oxcaml#6179](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6179), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6196](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6196), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6198](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6198), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6208](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6208), with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) handling sub-message location fixes

-**Runtime 4 removal** draft by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6172](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6172)); debug variable UID tracing draft by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) showing ~57% of typed variables reach debug info ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6158](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6158))
multicore: -**eio scheduler simplification**: [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened [ocaml-multicore/eio#849](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/849) moving read/write retry logic from scheduler into fiber-level code, removing special-cased `Read`/`Write` job types

-**`ftruncate`, `fdatasync`, and uring-based `fsync`** operations being added to `eio_linux` by [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) ([ocaml-multicore/eio#850](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/850))

-**ocaml-uring housekeeping**: C `assert` added for null CQE safety ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#148](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/148)), debug format specifiers corrected ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#146](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/146)), minimum OCaml version raised to 5.2 ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#150](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/150)), `Region.init` slot count now auto-derived ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#145](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/145))

weekly_summary.cross_repository_work:
- __RUMINANT:tools__ __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Dune 3.24.0 alpha cycle tracked across repositories**: [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) released alpha1 and alpha2 this week; [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) submitted dune 3.24.0~alpha2 (18 packages) to [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) ([ocaml/opam-repository#29996](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29996)); release notes indexed on [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) via the automated scraper ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3671](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3671)); dune 3.24's stanza removals forced `dune < 3.24` upper bounds into coq, rocq, and mdx packages in opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29988](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29988), [ocaml/opam-repository#29993](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29993))

- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Static linking convention proposal**: [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) opened [ocaml/opam-repository#29975](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29975) proposing `opam-packages-option-static-linking` convention packages; [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure) drew on MirageOS unikernel toolchain experience; the `dune-configurator` `?static` flag in [ocaml/dune#14947](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14947) is directly related to this ecosystem-wide static linking conversation

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Typechecker regression** on trunk — [ocaml/ocaml#14842](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14842) reports `Fatal error: exception Ctype.Unify(_)` affecting downstream packages including dolmen and containers; [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) are debugging level-escape and abbreviation-expansion issues in modular explicit types; the thread also saw [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) and [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron) raise concerns about LLM-generated text in bug reports diluting diagnostic information, with a suggestion to add guidance to `CONTRIBUTING.md`
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Implicit source positions RFC** ([ocaml/RFCs#52](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/52)) reached design consensus at the developer meeting: a single abstract `location` type is acceptable; [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere)'s alternative (option "1a + 2x") was not represented due to absence; updates planned for [ocaml/ocaml#13886](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13886)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Extensible IO channels** ([ocaml/ocaml#14775](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14775) by [Simon Cruanes](https://github.com/c-cube)) gathered developer meeting feedback on seekable channel handling, stdlib API surface, js_of_ocaml compatibility, and C FFI backward compatibility; soft consensus to allow extensible IO channels
- __RUMINANT:core__ **AST extension points** ([ocaml/ocaml#14668](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14668)) updated by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) to improve ppxlib's AST freeze strategy; [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) summarised developer meeting consensus to include an `extension` case for all syntactic categories without surface syntax initially
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Eager variant field unboxing in flambda2** — [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) noted in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6180](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6180) that flambda2 eagerly unboxes variant fields even without local allocation; proposed fix: only unbox fields with at least one use where the field is already available
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **`@ unique` destroy with interleaved reads** — [oxcaml/oxcaml#6146](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6146) (closed) discussed idiomatic pairing of `@ unique` destroy with C handle reads; conclusion: with-resource combinators or consume-and-return threading are current options; borrowing not yet available
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Box types** PR [oxcaml/oxcaml#5342](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/5342) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) continued review with [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) near-approving; open questions around GADT exhaustiveness and `mcomp` aliasability for `_ box`
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Topological ordering as long-term jkind solution** — [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) raised concerns in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6177](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/issues/6177); [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) outlined a future unification-based approach using layout variables
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **Kernel version gating for io_uring** — [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) raised in [ocaml-multicore/eio#845](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/845) whether `Eio_main` should restrict io_uring to kernel 7+ by default, given accumulating kernel bugs including a ZFS fixed-buffer regression
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **CLOEXEC socket flag defaulting** — [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) argued for always setting `CLOEXEC` for safety; [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) argued uring bindings should follow kernel defaults and Eio can apply the flag at a higher level ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#151](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/151))
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **`ocamlgrep` structural search via LSP** — [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) proposed integrating structural OCaml search via a custom LSP request in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1607); [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) questioned the fit, arguing LSP is designed around specific protocol interactions rather than whole-project analysis tools
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Lockfile invalidation from `conf-*` mutations** — [Yosef](https://github.com/yosefAlsuhaibani) reported in [ocaml/opam-repository#29985](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29985) that in-place changes to `conf-*` packages broke existing `opam lock` lockfiles; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) reverted and noted that locking virtual/conf packages is not fully supported by opam's design
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Proposal for `opam-packages-option-static-linking`** — [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) opened [ocaml/opam-repository#29975](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29975) proposing ecosystem-wide convention packages for static linking; [Calascibetta Romain](https://github.com/dinosaure), [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm), and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri) commented with MirageOS toolchain experience and proof-of-concept branches
